elements.text: (iv)Short sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure involving an eMortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: In States that require the return of a paper Note upon a short sale or deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure involving an eMortgage, the Seller/Servicer must provide the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: with a Seller/Servicer certified paper copy of the eNote (marked “Copy” and
elements.label: text

elements.text: “canceled”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: In such States, the Seller/Servicer must deliver a letter to the Borrower explaining that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower is receiving a paper copy of the eNote, originally registered in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: MERS eRegistry, and that the Seller/Servicer has caused the eNote to be de-activated
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the MERS eRegistry due to the eMortgage having been canceled as a result of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: short sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Foreclosure, bankruptcy or other legal proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: In States requiring the Seller/Servicer to be the holder of an eNote prior to commencing
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure or to take legal action (such as responding to the bankruptcy of a Borrower,
elements.label: text

elements.text: bringing or defending a lawsuit or other litigation relating to the maturity, prepayment,
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase, substitution, conversion, modification, or assumption of a Mortgage or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters with respect to an eMortgage), the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: custodian, as applicable, to request a change of Control of the eNote in the MERS
elements.label: text

elements.text: eRegistry from Freddie Mac to the Seller/Servicer and if needed, change of Location in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the MERS eRegistry from Freddie Mac or the Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote
elements.label: text

elements.text: custodian to the Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac is the eNote custodian, Form 1036 must be sent to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (Loan_Delivery_Funding_Ops@FreddieMac.com) at least three Business Days before
elements.label: text

elements.text: the change of Control is needed. Once the Seller/Servicer’s request is confirmed, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac or a Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodian, as applicable, will create an
elements.label: text

elements.text: offer of change of Control to the Seller/Servicer, which the Seller/Servicer must accept.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Immediately upon Seller/Servicer’s acceptances of the offer of change of Control:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. Freddie Mac or the applicable Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: shall be automatically and conclusively deemed to be the Seller/Servicer’s designated
elements.label: text

elements.text: custodian, as the term “designated custodian” is defined or described in the UETA
elements.label: text

elements.text: and E-SIGN, and will maintain the eNote in its eNote Vault System, in trust, for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: benefit of the Seller/Servicer; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. The Seller/Servicer shall be automatically and conclusively deemed to be the holder
elements.label: text

elements.text: of such eNote, as the term “holder” is defined in Article 1 of the UCC, as enacted in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the applicable State. The Seller/Servicer will be responsible for confirming that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer is identified as the Controller of the eNote in the MERS eRegistry
elements.label: text

elements.text: During the period for which the Seller/Servicer is identified as the Controller under this
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 1402.5(c)(v), Freddie Mac or the Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote
elements.label: text

elements.text: custodian, as applicable, will remain the Location in the MERS eRegistry and Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac or the applicable Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodian shall maintain
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Authoritative Copy of the eNote as the Seller/Servicer’s designated custodian, except
elements.label: text

elements.text: where the Location was transferred to the Seller/Servicer. Freddie Mac shall at all times,
elements.label: text

elements.text: however, be the owner or beneficial owner of the eNote.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the legal proceeding is concluded, the Seller/Servicer must promptly create an
elements.label: text

elements.text: offer of change of Control of the eNote in the MERS eRegistry to Freddie Mac and
elements.label: text

elements.text: change of Location to Freddie Mac or the Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote
elements.label: text

elements.text: custodian, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon Freddie Mac’s acceptance of the change of Control, Freddie Mac shall be the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Controller, and Freddie Mac or the Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodian, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, shall automatically and immediately cease maintaining the eNote, in trust, for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the benefit of the Seller/Servicer as its designated custodian and resume maintaining the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eNote for Freddie Mac. If, at the conclusion of the legal proceeding, the loan is charged
elements.label: text

elements.text: off, the Seller/Servicer must de-activate the eNote on the MERS eRegistry.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Producing the eNote for legal proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: other Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters with respect to an eMortgage, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must produce a Seller/Servicer certified paper copy of the eNote
elements.label: text

elements.text: (marked “Copy”) together with any required affidavit. The Seller/Servicer may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: convert the eNote into a paper note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Mortgage File Documents management
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Cross-referencing paper Mortgage File Documents to eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Paper documents that are part of the Mortgage File Documents related to an
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgage must be cross-referenced to all Electronic Records that are stored in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eNote Vault System for that eMortgage and, as necessary, the eStorage System. All
elements.label: text

elements.text: Records (both paper and Electronic) must be identified and cross-referenced to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable eMortgage. (See Section 1402.3(c)(iv) for eStorage System requirements.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Obtaining access to Custodial Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must complete and send Form 1036 to the Document Custodian to
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtain any Custodial Document in the Document Custodian’s custody.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Transfer of Servicing involving eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Transfer of Servicing involving eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Transferor Servicer may engage in a Transfer of Servicing involving eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided the Transferee Servicer is also an approved eMortgage Servicer for Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac. (Note: The approval process to service eMortgages is set forth in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Transfer of Servicing and custody
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with a Transfer of Servicing involving eMortgages, and in completing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1034T, the Transferor Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Deliver a Copy of the Authoritative Copy of eNote and eClosing Transaction Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Transferee Servicer’s eNote Vault System, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Change the “Servicer” designation in the MERS® System and the MERS eRegistry to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the legal name and change the MERS organizational identifier and MERS eRegistry
elements.label: text

elements.text: organizational identifier of the Transferee Servicer (who must have already received
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s approval to service eMortgages for Freddie Mac)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Transferee Servicer is using a Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: for eNote custody, the Transferor Servicer must create an offer of change in the MERS
elements.label: text

elements.text: eRegistry of the Location of the eNote to the Transferee Servicer’s eNote custodian.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Transferee Servicer is using Freddie Mac as the eNote custodian and Freddie Mac is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not already designated as the Location in the MERS eRegistry, the Transferor Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must create an offer of change in the MERS eRegistry of the Location of the eNote to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Transfer of Custodial Documents and Mortgage File Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to requesting Freddie Mac’s approval of a Transfer of Servicing involving
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgages, if some or all of the Mortgage File Documents are Electronic Records, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferor Servicer must confirm that the Transferee Servicer has an eNote Vault
elements.label: text

elements.text: System or other eStorage System capable of storing Electronic Mortgage File
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event the Transferee Servicer does not have a document and records
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintenance system capable of storing the Electronic Mortgage File Documents (other
elements.label: text

elements.text: than the Copy of the Authoritative Copy of the eNote), the Transferor Servicer must, in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 7101.5(a)1, either convert the documents and records to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: form of storage utilized by the Transferee Servicer or generate paper copies of all
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents and records for the Transferee Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Consistent with the requirements in Section 7101.4(c), the Transferor Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: instruct the Document Custodian responsible for storing and maintaining paper
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents related to all eMortgages connected to the Transfer of Servicing, to transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: such paper documents to the Transferee Servicer’s Document Custodian.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Special eMortgage Servicing representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer approved to service eMortgages for Freddie Mac represents and warrants
elements.label: text

elements.text: to, and agrees and covenants with, Freddie Mac that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer maintains (or causes its Servicing Agent to maintain) at all times
elements.label: text

elements.text: connectivity to the MERS® System, MERS eRegistry and MERS eDelivery
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer is at all times in compliance (or ensures that its Servicing Agent is in
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance) with the provisions of the MERS Membership Agreements and MERS Rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Membership
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer has not, and will not, through any act or omission on its part, cause
elements.label: text

elements.text: any eMortgage it services for Freddie Mac to become invalid, ineffective or
elements.label: text

elements.text: unenforceable or to lose its First Lien status
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Voluntary eMortgage repurchase request
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to complying with the Guide’s other repurchase requirements, when a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer makes a request to repurchase an eMortgage, the Seller/Servicer must identify
elements.label: text

elements.text: such eMortgage as an “eMortgage” in its request to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) New York CEMA Refinance of New York eMortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: A New York Consolidation, Extension and Modification Agreement Mortgage (“NYCEMA
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage”) may not be closed as an eMortgage and sold to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may however:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. Refinance eMortgages using the NYCEMA Mortgage process and documentation, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. Assist and cooperate with other lenders originating a refinance of eMortgages (serviced
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Servicer) that wish to use the NYCEMA Mortgage origination process and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation; provided that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Servicer requests a change of Control in the MERS® eRegistry from Freddie Mac to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer by sending a completed Form 1036 to Freddie Mac or the Freddie Mac￾approved third-party eNote custodian, as applicable, with the reason: “Servicer (or
elements.label: text

elements.text: another lender) is originating a NYCEMA refinance of an eMortgage serviced by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac is the eNote custodian, Form 1036 must be sent to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (Loan_Delivery_Funding_Ops@FreddieMac.com) at least three Business Days
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the change of Control is needed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. If such request is confirmed to be in compliance with Freddie Mac requirements,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac or a Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodian, as applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: will send an offer of change of Control of such eNote to Servicer via MERS eRegistry
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Servicer promptly accepts the offer of change of Control from Freddie Mac or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodian, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. After Servicer has accepted the offer of change of Control of the eNote, Servicer will
elements.label: text

elements.text: confirm that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The MERS eRegistry reflects that Servicer is the Controller, as Holder, of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: eNote, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac or a Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodian is the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Location
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. From the moment that Servicer accepts change of Control of such eNote, Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: shall have Control of the eNote and shall be the Controller of the eNote entitled to
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforce such eNote for the benefit of Freddie Mac, the eNote owner
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Servicer prints all pages of the eNote being refinanced under the NYCEMA Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: process to paper and certifies the paper copy is true, correct and complete in a blank
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Amendments to Purchase Documents

elements.text: amendment, or

elements.label: text

elements.text: amendment, The Seller’s delivery of Mortgages under a Purchase Document is
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and to the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Any other terms and conditions acceptable to Freddie Mac

elements.text: 1501.2: Purchase Contract provisions (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit toward a Commitment Amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Purchase tolerances stated in certain Pricing Identifier Terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Credit toward a Commitment Amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: The UPB of each Mortgage purchased pursuant to a Purchase Contract taken out under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guarantor or MultiLender Swap programs will be credited to the Commitment Amount,
elements.label: text

elements.text: continuing until the earlier of one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac has purchased Mortgages with an aggregate UPB equal to the Commitment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amount, subject to the allowable purchase tolerance, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Pricing Identifier Expiration Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless the Pricing Identifier Term provides otherwise, Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac under
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Cash Contract are eligible to fulfill the Seller’s Commitment Amount. At the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: request, the UPB of any Mortgages specified in a cash Purchase Contract may be credited to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller’s outstanding Commitment Amounts. If the Seller has two or more Pricing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Identifiers outstanding, the UPBs will be allocated to the Pricing Identifier specified in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash Contract under which the Mortgages are delivered to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Purchase tolerances stated in certain Pricing Identifier Terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase tolerances for Commitment Amounts are stated in certain Pricing Identifier Terms.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller delivers Mortgages with an aggregate UPB greater than the Commitment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amount plus the purchase tolerance, Freddie Mac reserves the right to charge an additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: fee for any Mortgages it agrees to purchase in excess of the Commitment Amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A cash Purchase Contract is subject to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The purchase tolerances applicable to fixed-rate Cash Contracts and ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contracts, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pairoff fees and pairoff procedures as set forth in Section 6401.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may pair off a mandatory Commitment Amount in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures set forth in Section 6401.1. Any pairoff fees assessed for failure to fulfill a
elements.label: text

elements.text: mandatory Commitment Amount shall be in addition to the pairoff fees assessed for failure to
elements.label: text

elements.text: satisfy the mandatory delivery requirements for fixed-rate Cash Contracts or for ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivered under cash Purchase Contracts.

elements.text: (a) Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers should be familiar with the following terms used in this section as they relate
elements.label: text

elements.text: to financial requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Agency Mortgage Servicing: The aggregate Residential First Lien Mortgage Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB of all Mortgages serviced for the Enterprises and Ginnie Mae
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Enterprise: Freddie Mac and Fannie Mae, each an “Enterprise” and collectively the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Enterprises.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Large Non-Depository Institutions: Non-depository institutions with $50 billion or
elements.label: text

elements.text: more in Residential First Lien Mortgage Servicing UPB plus Other Servicing UPB as
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined at the end of each calendar quarter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Liquidity: Cash and cash equivalents (unrestricted), certain unpledged investment grade
elements.label: text

elements.text: securities that are available for sale or held for trade (including single-family mortgage￾backed securities backed solely by Agency Mortgage Servicing, obligations of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Enterprises, and Treasury obligations), and 50% of committed/unused Agency Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing advance lines of credit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Long-Term Senior Unsecured Debt Rating: A rating assigned to a financial obligation
elements.label: text

elements.text: with an original maturity of one year or more that reflects both on the likelihood of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: default on contractually promised payments on senior unsecured debts and the expected
elements.label: text

elements.text: financial loss suffered in the event of default on such debts from a Rating Agency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Long-Term Corporate Family Rating: A long-term rating that reflects the relative
elements.label: text

elements.text: likelihood of a default on a corporate family’s debt and debt-like obligations and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expected financial loss suffered in the event of default from a Rating Agency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other Servicing UPB: Using the fields from the Form 1055, Mortgage Bankers’
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financial Reporting Form (MBFRF), the outstanding UPB of a Seller/Servicer’s portfolio
elements.label: text

elements.text: of one- to four- unit residential First Lien Mortgages the Seller/Servicer is contractually
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligated to service for all investors other than the Enterprises and Ginnie Mae, plus the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following regardless of the investor:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Second lien Mortgages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Funded home equity lines of credit,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Reverse Mortgages, plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Construction and land development Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: This does not include the outstanding UPB of Mortgages serviced under a subservicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: arrangement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rating Agency: An entity that is a “Nationally Recognized Statistical Rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: Organization” as defined by Section 78c(a) of Title 15 of the United States Code (15
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Residential First Lien Mortgage Servicing UPB: Using the fields from the Form 1055,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Bankers’ Financial Reporting Form (MBFRF), the outstanding UPB of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s portfolio of one- to four- unit residential First Lien Mortgages the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer is contractually obligated to service for the Enterprises and Ginnie Mae,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as applicable, excluding:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Funded home equity lines of credit,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Reverse Mortgages, plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Construction and land development Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: This does not include the outstanding UPB of Mortgages serviced under a subservicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: arrangement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer Rating: An evaluation of a servicer for its capacity to carry out servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: business, which is different from evaluations of financial instruments or credit standing
elements.label: text

elements.text: of corporations, from a Rating Agency. Servicer Ratings may be conducted based upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: the type of servicing actions performed, including evaluations for entities that act as
elements.label: text

elements.text: primary servicers, special servicers or master servicers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Small Seller: A Seller that originates $1 billion or less in one- to four- unit residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: first lien Mortgages (excluding reverse Mortgages, one-to four-unit residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: construction and land development loans to home buyers, and lot loans to consumers) in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the most recent four-quarter period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tangible Net Worth: Total equity (as determined by generally accepted accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: principles (GAAP)) less receivables due from related entities, less goodwill and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: intangible assets, less carrying value of pledged assets net of associated liabilities, less
elements.label: text

elements.text: deferred tax assets net of associated deferred tax liabilities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the deferred tax liabilities are greater than the deferred tax assets, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: deduction from the Tangible Net Worth will be zero.

elements.text: Worth of $2,500,000 plus a dollar amount equal to or exceeding the sum of:

elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Servicing UPB serviced for the Enterprises, if the Seller/Servicer remits

elements.text: (or an Enterprise draws) the interest and principal only as actually collected from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ 3.5 basis points of the Seller/Servicer’s Other Servicing UPB, plus

elements.text: ❑ 10 basis points of that portion of the Seller/Servicer’s Residential First Lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Servicing UPB serviced for Ginnie Mae
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An origination Liquidity* equal to or exceeding 50 basis points of the sum of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following as reported by Seller/Servicer using the fields from the Form 1055, Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bankers’ Financial Reporting Form (MBFRF):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ One-to-four unit residential first lien (excluding reverse Mortgages, one-to-four-unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: residential construction and land development loans to home buyers, and lot loans to
elements.label: text

elements.text: consumers) Mortgages held for sale, at lower of cost or market,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ One-to-four unit residential first lien (excluding reverse Mortgages, one-to-four-unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: residential construction and land development loans to home buyers, and lot loans to
elements.label: text

elements.text: consumers) Mortgages held for sale, at fair value, plus

elements.text: ❑ UPB of interest rate lock commitments after fallout adjustments.

elements.text: *Note: These origination Liquidity requirements do not apply to Small Sellers.

elements.text: (d) Seller/Servicers that are Large Non-Depository Institutions
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the Liquidity requirements specified in subsection (c) above, all

elements.text: 2101.3: Activity thresholds and no-activity fee
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Beginning January 1, 2014, a Seller/Servicer that does not meet certain activity thresholds may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be assessed a no-activity fee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To avoid being assessed the no-activity fee, Seller/Servicers must meet at least one of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following activity thresholds:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sell to Freddie Mac during the immediately preceding 36 months, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Service, or be a Servicing Agent for, a Mortgage portfolio for Freddie Mac as of December
elements.label: text

elements.text: 31 of the immediately preceding calendar year
elements.label: text

elements.text: New Seller/Servicers are exempt from the fee until they have been approved by Freddie Mac for
elements.label: text

elements.text: three years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The no-activity fee may also be assessed for each subsequent calendar year in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer does not meet the activity threshold but remains an approved Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer. The fee will be assessed and paid in accordance with the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 6303.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2101.4: Financial reporting (03/31/24)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: (a) All Seller/Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must at all times comply with the following reporting requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each Seller/Servicer must submit annual audited financial statements to Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: except that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A federally insured depository institution need not submit such statements, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Seller/Servicer that is approved only to sell Mortgages to Freddie Mac may submit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reviewed Financial Statements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each Seller/Servicer that is a subsidiary of another institution and that is required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit audited financial statements (as noted above) may submit audited financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: statements prepared at the Seller/Servicer level or at its parent level, with a consolidating
elements.label: text

elements.text: worksheet
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An annual audited report on internal controls is required only if audited financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: statements are required by Freddie Mac. This internal control report should be a separate
elements.label: text

elements.text: report stating whether the independent public accountant (IPA) noted any material
elements.label: text

elements.text: weaknesses during the audit of the financial statements. The report should be prepared in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Interpretation 1 of Statement and Auditing Standards (SAS) No. 60,
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Communication of Internal Control Structure Related Matters Noted in an Audit,” titled,
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Reporting on the Existence of Material Weaknesses” (AICPA, Professional Standards,
elements.label: text

elements.text: vol. I, AU sec. 9325).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If audited financial statements and internal control reports are required by Freddie Mac, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: audited financial statements and internal control reports must be prepared by an IPA who is a
elements.label: text

elements.text: licensed certified public accountant (CPA) or a public accountant licensed on or before
elements.label: text

elements.text: December 31, 1970, who complies with the applicable provisions of the public accountancy
elements.label: text

elements.text: law and rules of the jurisdiction(s) where the audit is conducted and the jurisdiction(s) in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the IPA is licensed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to require audited financial statements, audited reports on
elements.label: text

elements.text: internal controls, additional financial statements and other information relevant to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s eligibility, including audited or Reviewed Financial Statements for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s parent, subsidiaries and affiliates, at any time and regardless of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s annual eligibility reporting requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Seller/Servicers that are Large Non-Depository Institutions
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Large Non-Depository Institutions, as defined in Section 2101.2, must obtain an
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment of the Seller/Servicer’s performance and creditworthiness by a qualified,
elements.label: text

elements.text: independent third-party on an annual basis. The assessment must be made available to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac upon request, must substantiate that the Seller/Servicer has adequate capacity to
elements.label: text

elements.text: perform its financial obligations in an adverse stress environment and must meet the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following criteria:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ One primary Servicer Rating or master Servicing Rating, as applicable, as defined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 2101.2, for Large Non-Depository Institutions that have greater than or equal to
elements.label: text

elements.text: $50 billion in Servicing UPB; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ One primary Servicer Rating or master Servicing Rating, as applicable, and one third￾party Long-Term Senior Unsecured Debt Rating or Long-Term Corporate Family Rating,
elements.label: text

elements.text: each as defined in Section 2101.2, for Large Non-Depository Institutions that have
elements.label: text

elements.text: greater than $100 billion in Servicing UPB; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ One primary Servicer Rating or master Servicing Rating, as applicable, and issued by two
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rating Agencies, each of which must issue either a third-party Long-Term Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unsecured Debt Rating or Long-Term Corporate Family Rating for Large Non￾Depository Institutions that have greater than $150 billion in Servicing UPB
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, Large Non-Depository Institutions must submit to Freddie Mac, annually on or
elements.label: text

elements.text: before March 31, a capital and liquidity plan that describes how the Seller/Servicer intends to
elements.label: text

elements.text: manage its capital and liquidity consistent with Freddie Mac requirements. Capital and
elements.label: text

elements.text: liquidity plans must be submitted to CCRM_Mortgage_Bank@FreddieMac.com and must,
elements.label: text

elements.text: at a minimum:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include a description of the Seller/Servicer’s corporate governance over the capital and
elements.label: text

elements.text: liquidity planning process, such as oversight responsibilities of Senior Management and
elements.label: text

elements.text: its board of directors, and a discussion of the Seller/Servicer’s risk management
elements.label: text

elements.text: framework;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Describe processes to monitor and measure liquidity risks, such as business activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports and financial forecast and cashflow projections;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contain capital and liquidity contingency funding plans and provide for testing and
elements.label: text

elements.text: reaffirmation of such plans at least annually;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide for an annual liquidity stress test, including a stress test of the value of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract Rights in an adverse scenario (1) developed by the Seller/Servicer or (2) that
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be prescribed by Freddie Mac, or both. The Servicing Contract Rights stress test
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be conducted either in-house or using a third-party vendor; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Require notice to Freddie Mac within five (5) Business Days following any material
elements.label: text

elements.text: change to or material deviation from the plan. During times of stress, material changes
elements.label: text

elements.text: must require immediate notification within one (1) Business Day.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2101.5: Mortgage bankers financial reporting
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Each Seller/Servicer that is a mortgage banker must submit a complete and accurate Form 1055,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Bankers’ Financial Reporting Form, on a quarterly basis. For reporting purposes,
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage bankers are firms other than federally insured depositories originating mortgages for
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale in the secondary market and/or servicing mortgages. This includes Sellers/Servicers that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage banker subsidiaries of federally insured depositories.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1055 must be completed and submitted as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For reporting periods ending March 31, June 30 or September 30, Form 1055 must be filed
elements.label: text

elements.text: no later than 30 days after the end of the reporting period. For reporting periods ending on
elements.label: text

elements.text: December 31, the Form 1055 must be filed no later than 60 days after the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting period. Mortgage bankers with fiscal years that do not end on December 31 should
elements.label: text

elements.text: refer to the instructions for Form 1055 at http://www.mbfrf.org.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 1055 may be printed from the website and must be completed and submitted online at
elements.label: text

elements.text: http://www.mbfrf.org
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller/Servicer’s chief executive officer (CEO), chief financial officer (CFO) or an
elements.label: text

elements.text: equivalent officer must certify that Form 1055 was prepared in accordance with generally
elements.label: text

elements.text: accepted accounting principles (GAAP) and that the information submitted is true and
elements.label: text

elements.text: accurate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, for each Seller/Servicer that completes Form 1055 quarterly reporting and is either
elements.label: text

elements.text: (1) a Large Non-Depository Institution, as defined in Section 2101.2 or (2) separately identified
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac through a written communication to that Seller/Servicer, such Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must submit a complete and accurate Form 1055A, Monthly Mortgage Bankers’ Financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting Short Form, as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 1055A must be filed no later than the last day of the month following the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting month for the first and second months of each fiscal quarter (e.g., Form 1055A will
elements.label: text

elements.text: be effective for the April 2024 filing, due May 31, 2024). Seller/Servicers are not required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide a monthly report for each third month of the fiscal quarter, as the data will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: captured in the quarterly Form 1055 report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 1055A may be printed from the website and must be completed and submitted online at
elements.label: text

elements.text: http://www.mbfrf.org
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller/Servicer’s CEO, CFO or an equivalent officer must certify that Form 1055A was
elements.label: text

elements.text: prepared in accordance with GAAP and that the information submitted is true and accurate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Failure to submit Form 1055 or 1055A as described herein may result in suspension or
elements.label: text

elements.text: disqualification of the Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2101.6: Seller/Servicer insurance requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: (a) Coverage requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must maintain in effect, at all times and at its expense (i) a fidelity bond
elements.label: text

elements.text: and (ii) a mortgagee’s errors and omissions (E&O) insurance policy. The required limits and
elements.label: text

elements.text: deductibles/retentions for each coverage are set forth in Sections 2101.7 and 2101.8,
elements.label: text

elements.text: respectively.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller/Servicer uses a bond or policy that has been issued to its parent company, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted in Section 2101.6(c), the minimum limit of insurance must be based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: consolidated Base (as defined in Section 2101.7(c)) amount for the parent and for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: institutions related to the parent that are covered by the parent’s bond or policy, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will accept bonds or policies that provide for an aggregate limit of insurance for
elements.label: text

elements.text: a bond’s or policy’s term on the condition that the aggregate at least equals the minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: limit per loss or occurrence calculated in accordance with this Section 2101.7 or 2101.8, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller/Servicer is also a Freddie Mac Multifamily Seller/Servicer and coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: required under this Guide and the Freddie Mac Multifamily Seller/Servicer Guide is provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the same insurance contract, the higher minimum required coverage limit shall apply to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller/Servicer maintains broader coverage and/or higher limits than required by this
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide, Freddie Mac requires and shall be entitled to the benefits of the broader coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or higher limits maintained by the Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to adjust the insurance requirements for any particular
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer at Freddie Mac’s sole discretion.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Acceptable insurer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Coverage required under this section must be underwritten by an insurer that is rated A- (A￾minus) or better by the A.M. Best Company.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Parent institution’s coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Seller/Servicer is a subsidiary of an institution with insurance that meets Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s requirements set forth in Sections 2101.6 through 2101.9, Freddie Mac will accept the
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance of the Seller/Servicer’s parent as adequate for the Seller/Servicer if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer is named as joint insured, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Coverage under the parent’s policy or policies of insurance does not restrict or otherwise
elements.label: text

elements.text: limit the Seller/Servicer’s ability to comply with all of Freddie Mac’s insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Other obligations of the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must maintain at its offices a complete and accurate copy of its required
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance bonds and policies for the current year and the past five years. Copies of these
elements.label: text

elements.text: bonds and policies shall be provided to Freddie Mac upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer authorizes Freddie Mac to obtain copies of the bonds and policies and all
elements.label: text

elements.text: related information from the Seller/Servicer’s insurer and/or agent or representative of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s requirements for Seller/Servicer insurance do not diminish, restrict or
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise limit the Seller/Servicer’s responsibilities and obligations stated in the Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must familiarize itself with the terms of the required insurance coverages
elements.label: text

elements.text: and take all actions necessary to preserve the coverage and maximum benefits of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance for the Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event of bankruptcy where a stay is issued in relation to insurance coverage, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer is required to cooperate with Freddie Mac and agree to any stipulation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requested by Freddie Mac to lift the automatic stay in connection with Freddie Mac pursuing
elements.label: text

elements.text: its rights and/or benefits under any of the bonds and policies required by this Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any E&O policy provided on a “claims made” or “claims made and reported” basis, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must maintain continuous coverage dating back to the date on which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer became subject to this Guide. In the event of termination or non-renewal of
elements.label: text

elements.text: an E&O policy issued on a “claims made” or “claims made and reported” basis, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer shall either procure replacement coverage that provides for the same
elements.label: text

elements.text: continuity required by this section, or, if such continuity is not available in the marketplace,
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase an extended reporting period (sometimes referred to as tail coverage) on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: terminated/non-renewed coverage for six years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2101.7: Fidelity insurance coverage (06/08/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: (a) Documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fidelity insurance coverage may be documented on a bond form acceptable to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: or on the standard bond form currently mandated by or acceptable to the government agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: that has regulatory or supervisory authority over the Seller/Servicer. If the Seller/Servicer is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not regulated or supervised, Freddie Mac will accept coverage documented on the bond form
elements.label: text

elements.text: commonly issued to institutions similar to the Seller/Servicer. The coverage may be provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: in policy forms with names that include, but are not limited to, Fidelity Bond, Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bankers Bond, Financial Institution Bond, Financial Institution Crime Policy or Bankers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Blanket Bond. Whichever policy form is relied upon to document the coverage required by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, the Seller/Servicer warrants that the terms of such coverage meet all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Scope
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fidelity insurance coverage must protect the Seller/Servicer against loss resulting from
elements.label: text

elements.text: dishonesty, theft and/or fraud committed by any of the following persons (referred to as
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Designated Agents”), whether acting alone or in collusion with others:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any officer or employee of the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any employee of an outside firm while they are providing legal, data processing or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounting services for the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any person assigned to the Seller/Servicer through an intervening employer or agency to
elements.label: text

elements.text: perform the usual duties of an employee of the Seller/Servicer on a contingent or
elements.label: text

elements.text: temporary basis, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any intern of the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fidelity insurance must also include the following provisions for the benefit of Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as holder of an insurable interest in the Mortgages and/or REO originated, sold and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: serviced by the Seller/Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac is protected against any loss it incurs in connection with dishonesty, theft
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or fraud committed by any partner, sole proprietor or major shareholder of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer, whether acting alone or in collusion with others and without any
elements.label: text

elements.text: limitation as to improper personal gain
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac is named as a sole loss payee on payment drafts the insurer issues for losses
elements.label: text

elements.text: that Freddie Mac incurs in connection with acts covered by the insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac has the right to file a claim directly with the insurer if the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: fails to file a claim for a covered loss incurred by Freddie Mac when reasonably available
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bankruptcy or insolvency of the insured or of the insured’s estate will not relieve the
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurer of any of its obligations under the bond
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Limits and deductibles/retentions required
elements.label: text

elements.text: The minimum acceptable limits per fidelity loss or occurrence are listed in the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: table. The maximum amount of fidelity bond coverage required is $150 million.
