elements.text: 2101.8: Mortgagee’s errors and omissions (E&O)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: insurance coverage (06/08/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgagee’s errors and omissions (E&O) insurance coverage must be documented on policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: forms commonly issued to institutions similar to the Seller/Servicer. The coverage may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided in policy forms with names that include, but are not limited to, Mortgage Bankers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bond, Mortgage Errors & Omissions, Mortgage Impairment, Mortgagee Interest, Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Holders Liability, Professional Liability or Mortgage Protection. Whichever policy form is
elements.label: text

elements.text: relied upon to document the coverage required by Freddie Mac, the Seller/Servicer warrants
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the terms of such coverage meet all of the requirements in this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Scope
elements.label: text

elements.text: The coverage must protect the Seller/Servicer against loss resulting from negligence, errors
elements.label: text

elements.text: or omissions committed by the Seller/Servicer or any Designated Agent (as defined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 2101.7(b)) while performing, at a minimum, the following duties with respect to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages originated and/or sold by the Seller and/or Mortgages or REO serviced by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determining whether the Mortgaged Premises are located in Special Flood Hazard Areas
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensuring that the Mortgaged Premises are insured in accordance with Chapter 4703
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintaining, where applicable, FHA insurance, VA guaranty, RHS guaranty or mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance on the Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complying with applicable reporting requirements of the FHA, VA, RHS or MI, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensuring the payment of property taxes and any other mandatory assessments on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises or REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: E&O insurance coverage must also include the following provisions for the benefit of Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, as holder of an insurable interest in the Mortgages and/or REO originated, sold and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: serviced by the Seller/Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac is named as a loss payee on payment drafts the insurer issues for losses that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac incurs in connection with acts covered by the insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac has the right to file a claim directly with the insurer if the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: fails to file a claim for a covered loss incurred by Freddie Mac when reasonably available
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bankruptcy or insolvency of the insured or of the insured’s estate will not relieve the
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurer of any of its obligations under the policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Limits and deductibles
elements.label: text

elements.text: E&O coverage must equal the amount of the Seller/Servicer’s fidelity bond coverage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, Freddie Mac does not require E&O coverage in excess of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ $10 million if the Base (as defined in Section 2101.8(c)) consists only of Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ $30 million if the Base consists of Home Mortgages and Multifamily Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will accept E&O policies providing coverage per Mortgage if the insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: limit per Mortgage is no less than the UPB of the largest Mortgage originated and/or sold by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer, whichever is highest, provided that Seller/Servicer must review the
elements.label: text

elements.text: balances of the Mortgages before each premium renewal date to determine whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: above limitation needs to be increased as the result of the origination of higher balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages during the last coverage period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For policies that provide coverage per Mortgage loan, the maximum deductible amount for
elements.label: text

elements.text: each Mortgage loan cannot be more than 5% of the insurer’s liability per Mortgage loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For policies that provide coverage per aggregate loss, the deductible is determined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the following table:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Base* Maximum deductible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Less than $1 billion The greater of $100,000 or 10% of the face amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: Equal to or greater than $1 billion 15% of the face amount of the policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: * Base = The highest of total annual Home Mortgage and Multifamily Mortgage origination, sale or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing volume, including Home Mortgages and Multifamily Mortgages held in portfolio. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must include in its Base the UPB of all Mortgages for which it holds the related Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract Right, regardless of whether a Servicing Agent has been engaged to perform subservicing on
elements.label: text

elements.text: behalf of the Seller/Servicer. A Servicing Agent must only include in its Base the UPB of Mortgages for
elements.label: text

elements.text: which it holds the related Servicing Contract Right and need not include the UPB of Mortgages for which
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicing Agent has been engaged to perform subservicing on behalf of another Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2101.9: Seller/Servicer insurance reporting
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: requirements (06/08/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Reporting of insurance documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within 30 days of obtaining or renewing any required bond or policy, the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must submit to Freddie Mac (see Directory 1) a certificate of insurance and related
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation showing the following information:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Name and address of insurer and insurance broker
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bond or policy number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer as named insured or joint named insured
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Type of insurance and coverage; limit of liability on a per loss, occurrence or mortgage,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or claims-made basis (specify) and/or any applicable aggregate limit of coverage; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective dates of coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deductible or retention amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any endorsement or optional coverage modifying the original bond or policy if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: endorsement or optional coverage reinforces compliance with Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements or effectively reduces the coverage required by Freddie Mac. Reference to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: form number is not acceptable. A copy of the endorsement or optional coverage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: required if the endorsement or optional coverage cannot be summarized substantively on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The insurer’s agreement to notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 1):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ At least 30 calendar days before the insurer, on its own initiative, cancels or non￾renews the Seller/Servicer’s coverage for any reason
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Within 10 Business Days after the insurer, on its own initiative, reduces or
elements.label: text

elements.text: restrictively modifies the Seller/Servicer’s coverage for any reason or, at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s request, cancels or non-renews the Seller/Servicer’s coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Reporting of loss or change in coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must report to Freddie Mac the following events within 10 Business Days
elements.label: text

elements.text: of their occurrence using the Change and Activity Report, submitted in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 2101.12:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The determination that any single act covered under the required bonds and policies has
elements.label: text

elements.text: caused loss exceeding $100,000, whether or not Freddie Mac’s interests are affected or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: claim is filed with the insurer, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The receipt of notice from any insurer that such insurer has taken or intends to take action
elements.label: text

elements.text: to cancel, reduce, not renew or restrictively modify any required Seller/Servicer insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: coverage for any reason. The Seller/Servicer must include a copy of the insurer’s notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: and detail the reasons for the insurer’s action or intended action if not stated in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurer’s notice. The Seller/Servicer must also report its effort to obtain replacement
elements.label: text

elements.text: coverage or otherwise satisfy Freddie Mac’s insurance requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2101.10: Annual certification (12/07/20)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Freddie Mac makes the Annual Certification available to each Seller/Servicer on or about the end
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Seller/Servicer’s fiscal year. The Annual Certification Report requires certain information
elements.label: text

elements.text: about the Seller/Servicer and its operations and the Seller/Servicer’s certification that it continues
elements.label: text

elements.text: to meet Freddie Mac’s eligibility requirements and comply with the provisions and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Guide and the Seller/Servicer’s other Purchase Documents. If the Seller/Servicer does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: meet or comply with one or more requirements, it must identify each such failure on the Annual
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certification Report together with such information concerning remediation of such failure as
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 2101.11 for information and requirements regarding submission of the Annual
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certification Report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2101.11: Annual reporting requirements (12/07/20)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following must be completed with information as of the Seller/Servicer’s fiscal year-end and
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted to Freddie Mac within 90 days after the end of that fiscal year:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Annual Certification Report (see Section 2101.10)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Audited or reviewed financial statements, as required by Section 2101.4, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other documentation requested by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must access and complete an Annual Certification Report using the Unified
elements.label: text

elements.text: Counterparty Experience® (UCount®) system and any required audited financial statements and
elements.label: text

elements.text: auditor’s reports (see Section 2101.4) must be submitted using UCount or electronically as
elements.label: text

elements.text: attachment to an e-mail to Institutional_Eligibility@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer is responsible for truthfulness, completeness and accuracy of the information
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the electronically executed and submitted Annual Certification Report (including any required
elements.label: text

elements.text: or supplemental information submitted with the Annual Certification Report) to the same degree
elements.label: text

elements.text: as if the Seller/Servicer’s duly authorized representative made the submission to Freddie Mac in
elements.label: text

elements.text: paper and signed in ink.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer that is suspended from selling Mortgages to or Servicing Mortgages for, or is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not approved to sell Mortgages to or service Mortgages for Freddie Mac is not required to submit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Annual Certification Report unless directed to do so by Freddie Mac. Failure of any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer to submit the Annual Certification Report as set forth in this Section 2101.11 may
elements.label: text

elements.text: result in suspension or disqualification of that Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2101.12: Reporting of changes in Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: organization or status (05/13/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must submit a Change and Activity Report to Freddie Mac with respect to any
elements.label: text

elements.text: material change in its ownership or organization (“Material Organizational Change”) using the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unified Counterparty Experience® (UCount®) system at least 60 calendar days before the
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective date of such Material Organizational Change.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Material Organizational Changes include, but are not limited to, any of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Merger (whether the Seller/Servicer is the surviving entity or non-surviving entity)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Consolidation (including a regulatory agency-assisted transaction)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Change of Control
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) A conversion or change in the Seller/Servicer’s legal structure (such as a corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: becoming a limited liability company), legal jurisdiction (such as a change of State of
elements.label: text

elements.text: formation) or charter (such as a State-chartered bank becoming federally chartered)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Transfer of all or substantially all of the Seller/Servicer’s assets to another entity
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to submitting a Change and Activity Report, as a condition to obtaining or
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintaining a Seller/Servicer’s eligibility status as a Freddie Mac-approved Seller/Servicer, any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Material Organizational Change (including (a) through (e) described above) requires Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s prior written approval or notice of non-objection before the effective date of the Material
elements.label: text

elements.text: Organizational Change. Freddie Mac may require a Seller/Servicer to satisfy certain conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: in connection with Freddie Mac’s prior written approval or notice of non-objection (including,
elements.label: text

elements.text: without limitation, assumption by the surviving entity of the liabilities of the non-surviving
elements.label: text

elements.text: entity, posting of collateral, satisfaction of FRE Claims, execution and delivery of agreements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with any of the foregoing and/or submission of any additional information or
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation related to the Material Organizational Change). Freddie Mac’s decision whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: to grant approval or provide notice of non-objection, and whether to impose any conditions in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with the foregoing, with respect to any Material Organizational Change will be in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s sole and absolute discretion.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with any Material Organizational Change, the following shall constitute a breach
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Guide and an “Unauthorized Transaction”: (i) Seller/Servicer’s failure to timely submit a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Change and Activity Report to Freddie Mac in connection therewith, or (ii) the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: consummates such Material Organizational Change without Freddie Mac’s prior written
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval or notice of non-objection. In the event of an Unauthorized Transaction, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: shall have the right to assess a compensatory fee determined by Freddie Mac in its sole and
elements.label: text

elements.text: absolute discretion and all other rights and remedies under the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Material Organizational Change results in a Transfer of Servicing, whether by operation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: law or otherwise, the applicable Seller/Servicers must comply with their respective obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Guide and applicable Purchase Documents, including, but not limited to, Sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1101.3, 2101.14 and Chapter 7101. If there is a Transfer of Servicing, the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must obtain Freddie Mac’s prior written approval in accordance with Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7101 for the proposed transaction(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Seller/Servicer intends to (i) transfer assets to an entity that is not a Freddie Mac-approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer or (ii) merge or consolidate with and into an entity that is not a Freddie Mac￾approved Seller/Servicer, see Section 2101.13 for additional requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must submit a Change and Activity Report to Freddie Mac using UCount no
elements.label: text

elements.text: later than one Business Day after any of the following events:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. A name change, including a change relating to a doing-business-as, fictitious or alias name
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. A change concerning:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Relationships with any Affiliate
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) External auditors
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Fiscal year-end
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Any contract between a Seller/Servicer and a Related Third Party that will have or is
elements.label: text

elements.text: reasonably likely to have a Material Adverse Effect
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. A change in Seller/Servicer’s headquarter address, registered agent address or principal place
elements.label: text

elements.text: of business or a change in the location of its books and records (other than a non-material
elements.label: text

elements.text: change in the location of its books and records in the normal course of its business in selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: or servicing Mortgages)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. A change in Senior Management
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must submit a Change and Activity Report to Freddie Mac using UCount
elements.label: text

elements.text: within one Business Day after it:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Is terminated by a mortgage insurance company, secondary marketing agency or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: secondary mortgage investor
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Voluntarily files a petition under federal bankruptcy or State insolvency laws, or answers an
elements.label: text

elements.text: involuntary proceeding admitting insolvency or inability to pay debts
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Fails to obtain a vacation or stay of involuntary proceedings brought for its reorganization,
elements.label: text

elements.text: dissolution or liquidation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Is adjudged bankrupt or insolvent
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Has a trustee, conservator or receiver appointed for the Seller/Servicer or its property
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Makes an assignment for the benefit of its creditors
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Is put on probation or has its activities restricted in any manner by any agency of the federal
elements.label: text

elements.text: or of State government
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Becomes subject to an active or threatened (overtly and in writing) class action legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeding or a regulatory or supervisory action, proceeding or investigation including,
elements.label: text

elements.text: without limitation, any judgment, order, finding or settlement that may have a Material
elements.label: text

elements.text: Adverse Effect
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. With respect to any lender or secured party of a credit, loan or funding facility, including, but
elements.label: text

elements.text: not limited to, any Warehouse Lender or any Secured Party under an Acknowledgment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement or Consent Agreement:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Notifies any lender or secured party, or is notified by any lender or secured party of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: breach or default under a credit, loan or funding facility or other related financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents, or of Seller/Servicer’s violation of any financial covenants (e.g., covenants
elements.label: text

elements.text: relating to Seller/Servicer’s working capital, debt or equity or net worth) of a credit, loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: or funding facility or other related financing documents (to the extent such breach,
elements.label: text

elements.text: default or violation is not cured within any applicable cure period set forth in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable agreement); or
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Notifies the Warehouse Lender or Secured Party, or is notified by the Warehouse Lender
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Secured Party of a breach or default under a Warehouse Agreement or other relevant
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract (including an Acknowledgment Agreement or Consent Agreement), or of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s violation of any financial covenant (e.g., covenants relating to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s working capital, debt or equity, servicing portfolio or net worth) of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Warehouse Agreement or other relevant contract (including an Acknowledgment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement or Consent Agreement); or
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Agrees to or becomes aware of any other major event regarding the agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: referenced in the foregoing Guide Section 2101.12(9)(a), such as execution of a new
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement or cancelation, termination or substantive modification to an existing
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement or other relevant contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10. Without derogating from the generality of Section 2101.12(8), with respect to any dispute,
elements.label: text

elements.text: litigation or other adversary proceeding with a Related Third Party or other party that may
elements.label: text

elements.text: have a Material Adverse Effect:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Receives notice of a dispute not subject to litigation or other adversary proceeding, no
elements.label: text

elements.text: later than one Business Day after Seller/Servicer’s reasonable determination that such
elements.label: text

elements.text: dispute may have a Material Adverse Effect
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Initiates litigation or other adversary proceedings asserting claims by or on behalf of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer that may have a Material Adverse Effect, no later than one Business Day
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the initiation of such litigation or proceedings; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Receives notice of litigation or other adversary proceedings asserting claims against the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer that may have a Material Adverse Effect, no later than one Business Day
elements.label: text

elements.text: after notice to the Seller/Servicer of any such litigation or proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must keep Freddie Mac apprised in writing of any major
elements.label: text

elements.text: developments in any such dispute, litigation or other adversary proceedings and must
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide, at Freddie Mac’s request, copies of any pleadings or other documents related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the dispute, litigation or other adversary proceedings. Freddie Mac may also require the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer to provide additional information concerning any event covered by this
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 2101.12(10) and reserves the right to take any action it deems necessary based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: such information and change.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 11. Receives notice that the Seller/Servicer is suspended, disqualified or terminated as a Fannie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mae-approved seller, lender or servicer and/or a Ginnie Mae-approved issuer
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with the submission of any Change and Activity Report or Material Organizational
elements.label: text

elements.text: Change, Freddie Mac in its sole discretion may request (and the Seller/Servicer must promptly
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide) additional information on such change or activity, including, without limitation,
elements.label: text

elements.text: organizational charts or any or all of the documentation set forth in Section 2101.1(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer submitting a Change and Activity Report should determine if a new Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Incumbency (and board of directors or other governing body resolution) should be submitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: with new or modified fund transfer instructions or other payment instructions. See Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2201.1 for forms and delivery instructions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2101.13: Transfer of assets to an entity that is not a
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Freddie Mac-approved Seller/Servicer (12/07/20)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Failure to comply with all of the requirements of this Section 2101.13 within the prescribed time
elements.label: text

elements.text: frame may result in suspension or disqualification of the Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must complete a Change and Activity Report and submit relevant information
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac using the Unified Counterparty Experience® (UCount®) system at least 75
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Days prior to the proposed transfer of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All of its assets without the transfer of accompanying liabilities, to an entity that is not a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac-approved Seller/Servicer, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The assets of a Mortgage-related function (for example, originations or Servicing), without
elements.label: text

elements.text: the transfer of accompanying liabilities, to an entity that is not a Freddie Mac-approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: In such situations, Freddie Mac does not consider that the Seller/Servicer has transferred its
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac approval and Seller/Servicer number to the transferee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may require the Seller/Servicer to provide additional information concerning the
elements.label: text

elements.text: proposed transfer of assets.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The proposed transferee must obtain Freddie Mac’s approval as a Seller/Servicer prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective date of the asset transfer. To obtain such approval, the proposed transferee must apply
elements.label: text

elements.text: to become an approved Seller/Servicer concurrent with the Seller/Servicer’s submission of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Change and Activity Report. If the proposed transferee fails receive approval as a Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer, it will be precluded from selling Mortgages to or Servicing Mortgages for,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac. Information on applying for approval as a Freddie Mac Seller/Servicer may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained by contacting Freddie Mac (see Directory 1) or by visiting:
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/working-with-us/become-a-seller-servicer/overview.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2101.14: Seller/Servicer changes that result in a
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Transfer of Servicing (12/07/20)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A change in a Seller/Servicer’s organization may also involve a Transfer of Servicing. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: change will result in a Transfer of Servicing as defined in the Glossary, the Seller/Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with the notification requirements in Sections 2101.12 and 2101.13, and all requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Subsequent Transfers of Servicing in Chapter 7101, including the requirement that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: request for approval of the transfer be submitted to Freddie Mac at least 45 Business Days prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the requested Effective Date of Transfer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a proposed transfer of assets includes a Transfer of Servicing, the request for approval of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: proposed Transfer of Servicing should be submitted along with the application to become a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer, but no later than 45 Business Days prior to the Effective Date of the Transfer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Transfer of Servicing will result from an organizational or status change, the request for
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval of Transfer of Servicing must be submitted 45 Business Days prior to the requested
elements.label: text

elements.text: Effective Date of Transfer even though the notification of the organizational change is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: required until after the change has occurred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2101.15: Seller/Servicer responsibilities regarding
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Related Third Parties (05/06/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Aggregators and the sale of Wholesale Home Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Approval required
elements.label: text

elements.text: In order to sell Wholesale Home Mortgages to Freddie Mac, an institution must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved by Freddie Mac as a Seller of Wholesale Home Mortgages. This approval is
elements.label: text

elements.text: separate from the approvals referenced in Section 2101.1(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may require that the Seller provide additional information and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation relating to the Seller and its Wholesale Home Mortgage program, policies
elements.label: text

elements.text: and procedures and satisfy certain conditions in connection with Freddie Mac’s approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Responsibility and oversight
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to Wholesale Home Mortgages sold by an Aggregator to Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: although certain functions described in the Purchase Documents may be performed by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Third-Party Originator, the Aggregator is responsible for compliance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of all Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As part of such responsibility, the Aggregator is responsible for ensuring that such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages satisfy general eligibility requirements (see Chapter 4201) and comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: all requirements of all applicable federal, State and local laws, rules and regulations (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4202.1).
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the requirements of Section 4201.8, the Aggregator must operate a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Wholesale Home Mortgage oversight program that is acceptable to Freddie Mac. Such
elements.label: text

elements.text: program must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be formally documented
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that all Wholesale Home Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac have been subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: a quality control program that satisfies the applicable requirements set forth in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 3402.1(a) and that employs a stratified sampling methodology to ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: adequate representation of Mortgages related to Third-Party Originators
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that Third-Party Originators that use Electronic Loan Documents satisfy the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable requirements set forth in Section 1401.3(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide for the maintenance of records and information relating to each Third-Party
elements.label: text

elements.text: Originator, including up-to-date pipeline information regarding committed and in￾process Mortgages relating to such Third-Party Originator
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure privacy or cyber events reported by Third-Party Originators are reported to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac within 36 hours in accordance with guidelines outlined in Chapter 1302
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include a process for the documented due diligence and oversight of all Third-Party
elements.label: text

elements.text: Originators, which due diligence must include, but not be limited to, review of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Most recent financial statements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Current permits, licenses and regulatory approvals
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Resumes or other relevant information of principal officers managing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting and originating process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Results of background checks for principal officers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If it has access to any Freddie Mac System (as defined in Section 2401.1(b)),
elements.label: text

elements.text: information security practices that satisfy the applicable requirements set forth in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 1302
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Fraud risk management processes and controls that satisfy the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements set forth in Chapter 3201, including processes and controls to ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: timely notification and reporting to the Seller of any activity outlined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: 2101.15: Seller/Servicer responsibilities regarding
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Related Third Parties (Future effective date 08/05/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Aggregators and the sale of Wholesale Home Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Approval required
elements.label: text

elements.text: In order to sell Wholesale Home Mortgages to Freddie Mac, an institution must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved by Freddie Mac as a Seller of Wholesale Home Mortgages. This approval is
elements.label: text

elements.text: separate from the approvals referenced in Section 2101.1(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may require that the Seller provide additional information and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation relating to the Seller and its Wholesale Home Mortgage program, policies
elements.label: text

elements.text: and procedures and satisfy certain conditions in connection with Freddie Mac’s approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Responsibility and oversight
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to Wholesale Home Mortgages sold by an Aggregator to Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: although certain functions described in the Purchase Documents may be performed by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Third-Party Originator, the Aggregator is responsible for compliance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of all Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As part of such responsibility, the Aggregator is responsible for ensuring that such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages satisfy general eligibility requirements (see Chapter 4201) and comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: all requirements of all applicable federal, State and local laws, rules and regulations (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4202.1).
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the requirements of Section 4201.8, the Aggregator must operate a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Wholesale Home Mortgage oversight program that is acceptable to Freddie Mac. Such
elements.label: text

elements.text: program must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be formally documented
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that all Wholesale Home Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac have been subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: a quality control program that satisfies the applicable requirements set forth in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 3402.1(a) and that employs a stratified sampling methodology to ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: adequate representation of Mortgages related to Third-Party Originators
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that Third-Party Originators that use Electronic Loan Documents satisfy the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable requirements set forth in Section 1401.3(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide for the maintenance of records and information relating to each Third-Party
elements.label: text

elements.text: Originator, including up-to-date pipeline information regarding committed and in￾process Mortgages relating to such Third-Party Originator
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure privacy or cyber events reported by Third-Party Originators to Aggregators
elements.label: text

elements.text: are reported to Freddie Mac within 36 hours of receiving notice in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: guidelines outlined in Chapter 1302
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that neither the Third-Party Originator nor its Senior Management is on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exclusionary List with respect to any related Wholesale Home Mortgage sold to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include and execute a process for the documented due diligence of all Third-Party
elements.label: text

elements.text: Originators, which due diligence must include, but not be limited to, review of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Most recent financial statements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Current permits, licenses and regulatory approvals
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Resumes or other relevant information of principal officers managing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting and originating process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Background checks for Senior Management
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Information security programs with documented processes and controls to
elements.label: text

elements.text: manage Incidents (as defined in Section 1302.5(a)) and other risk events and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure timely notification and reporting to the Aggregator Incidents and other risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: event, and if a Third-Party Originator has access to any Freddie Mac System (as
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined in Section 2401.1(b)), such program must incorporate documented
elements.label: text

elements.text: processes and controls substantially similar to those required of Seller/Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: under Chapter 1302
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Fraud risk management programs that incorporate documented processes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: controls substantially similar to those required of Seller/Servicers under Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3201, including processes and controls to detect and identify fraud, suspected
elements.label: text

elements.text: fraud and other Suspicious Activity and ensure timely notification and reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Aggregator of any activity outlined in Section 3201.2(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon request of Freddie Mac, the Aggregator must promptly provide to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: information and documentation relating to its Wholesale Home Mortgage oversight
elements.label: text

elements.text: program and any requested pipeline information, records or due diligence documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to Third-Party Originators.

elements.text: Chapter 2201: Additional Documentation
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2201.1: Seller/Servicer Certificate of Incumbency
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: requirements (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller/Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Related Third Parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Seller/Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Seller/Servicer must provide Freddie Mac with a certificate of incumbency
elements.label: text

elements.text: (“Certificate of Incumbency” or “COI”) using one of the following forms:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 988SF, Certificate of Incumbency: for Banks, Savings Banks, Savings and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Associations, Credit Unions, Corporations or Limited Liability Companies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 988SF – HFA, Certificate of Incumbency: for Housing Finance Agencies or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 989SF, Certificate of Incumbency: for Limited Partnerships
elements.label: text

elements.text: The COI must be accompanied by a resolution (“Resolution”) of the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: board of directors, board of members, sole member, general partner or other governing
elements.label: text

elements.text: body (“Governing Body”) that empowers and authorizes certain employees to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: individually and singularly, provide Freddie Mac with instructions (or modified
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions) to transfer funds or securities by wire transfer, Automated Clearing House
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ACH) or other funds transfer system expressly approved and designated by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: in connection with the sale of Mortgages to and/or Servicing of Mortgages for Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac (such instructions or modified instructions, “Wire Transfer Instructions”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: A certified copy of the Seller/Servicer’s Resolution, in the form provided electronically
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Seller/Servicer by Freddie Mac unless applicable law requires otherwise, must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: attached to the executed COI as Exhibit A. For each duly authorized employee
elements.label: text

elements.text: (“Authorized Employee”), the COI must contain either such Authorized Employee’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: original pen and ink signature or an Electronic copy or representation of the original pen
elements.label: text

elements.text: and ink signature.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Delivery and execution of COI and required documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Delivery of COI and associated documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: The COI, Resolution and any other required documents or forms must be delivered to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac either as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Paper Records, as defined in Section 1401.1(b), signed by the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Secretary, Assistant Secretary or in-house counsel (a “Signatory”) using a pen and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ink, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic Records, as defined in Section 1401.1(b), using a Portable Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Format (PDF) with a copy or representation of the pen and ink signature of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s Signatory attached thereto or logically associated therewith as an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Signature, as defined in Section 1401.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer agrees that delivery of these documents as Electronic Records to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, with the Electronic Signature of the Seller/Servicer’s Signatory attached
elements.label: text

elements.text: thereto or logically associated therewith, is as valid, effective and enforceable as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s delivery of an original paper Record of such documents signed using
elements.label: text

elements.text: a pen and ink. Freddie Mac and the Seller/Servicer agree that delivery of Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Records with Electronic Signatures is an Eligible Electronic Transaction, as defined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 1401.1(b) and governed by Chapter 1401.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Any such Electronic Record must be a PDF attached to an e-mail and delivered
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac at Counterparty_Authorization@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Seller/Servicer representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: By executing the COI and delivering it and the Resolution to Freddie Mac, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer represents and warrants to, and covenants with, Freddie Mac that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each person listed under paragraph numbered 2 of the COI is the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: employee who has been (and continues to be) duly authorized by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s Governing Body as an Authorized Employee, who is
elements.label: text

elements.text: individually and singularly empowered to provide Freddie Mac with Wire
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer Instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each Authorized Employee (a) holds the position or title as an employee of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer that is set forth directly across from the employee’s name and (b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: is duly authorized individually and singularly to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Execute any and all paper Records or Electronic Records, instruments,
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents or forms that are necessary or proper or required by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: to effectuate the authority set forth in paragraph numbered 1 of the COI by
elements.label: text

elements.text: having the Authorized Employee either sign paper Records with a pen and ink
elements.label: text

elements.text: or electronically sign Electronic Records by attaching or logically associating
elements.label: text

elements.text: a copy or representation of the Authorized Employee’s pen and ink signature
elements.label: text

elements.text: as the Authorized Employee’s Electronic Signature, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Deliver the duly executed paper Record or Electronic Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The specimen signature set forth directly across from each Authorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: Employee’s name and position or title is the true and genuine original pen and ink
elements.label: text

elements.text: signature or Electronic Signature of each such Authorized Employee, and Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac may conclusively rely on the accuracy, genuineness, good faith, validity,
elements.label: text

elements.text: effectiveness and enforceability of any paper Record or Electronic Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided to Freddie Mac bearing an Authorized Employee’s signature
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each Authorized Employee is duly authorized to engage in and conduct
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Transactions, as defined in Chapter 1401, to provide Freddie Mac with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Wire Transfer Instructions, and the Seller/Servicer consents to such Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each COI is a Purchase Document and all capitalized terms used in such forms
elements.label: text

elements.text: that are not defined therein, shall have the meanings ascribed to such terms in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The information set forth in any COI delivered to Freddie Mac is true and correct,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Freddie Mac may conclusively rely upon the information in the COI and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: addendum, Resolution, paper Record or Electronic Record attached to (or
elements.label: text

elements.text: associated with) the COI that is delivered to Freddie Mac until the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivers a new COI to Freddie Mac that is reviewed and accepted by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to verify or authenticate any Seller/Servicer request for
elements.label: text

elements.text: a change, and the Seller/Servicer may not consider Freddie Mac’s failure to do so an
elements.label: text

elements.text: act of negligence.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Removal of authorized employees
elements.label: text

elements.text: In an urgent situation, the Seller/Servicer may request Freddie Mac to remove the name
elements.label: text

elements.text: of an employee who is listed as an Authorized Employee from the Seller/Servicer’s COI
elements.label: text

elements.text: by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sending an e-mail or facsimile transmission with a copy of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A cover letter signed by a Signatory directing Freddie Mac to remove an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorized Employee’s name and position or title from the list of Authorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: Employees, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A copy of the Seller/Servicer’s most recent COI provided to Freddie Mac with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorized Employee’s name struck through (lined-out) (see Directory 8)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Promptly delivering a new COI together with any new Wire Transfer Instructions, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will make every effort to act promptly on such urgent requests to remove
elements.label: text

elements.text: the name and position or title of such Authorized Employee but cannot guarantee that it
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be able to prevent wire transfers of funds or securities made in accordance with Wire
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer Instructions previously provided to Freddie Mac by such Authorized Employee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Continuity and validation Wire Transfer Instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Authorized Employee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any Wire Transfer Instructions provided by an Authorized Employee on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s behalf remain in effect and are valid until modified by such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorized Employee or another Authorized Employee. The Seller/Servicer is solely
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsible for any and all Wire Transfer Instructions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller/Servicer has requested that Freddie Mac remove an Authorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: Employee from its COI, the Seller/Servicer should review all existing Wire Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instructions to determine if any other changes are necessary.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Sole Authorized Employee
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Seller/Servicer has requested that Freddie Mac remove the sole Authorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: Employee assigned to the Loan Selling Advisor® user role of Setup Manager from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s COI, the Seller/Servicer must concurrently assign the role of Setup
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manager to another Authorized Employee on the Seller/Servicer’s COI.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, if the Seller/Servicer has requested that Freddie Mac remove from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s COI the Authorized Employee(s) who executed the most recently
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivered Form 483, Wire Transfer Instructions; Form 987E, Wire Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorization for a Cash Warehouse Delivery; Form 1132, Authorization for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automatic Transfer of Funds Through the Automated Clearing House (ACH) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers; or Form 1132A, Authorization for Automatic Transfer of Funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: from Principal and Interest Custodial Account through the Automated Clearing House
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ACH), the Seller/Servicer must submit new Forms 483, 987E, 1132 and/or 1132A,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as applicable, signed by an Authorized Employee on the Seller COI.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to the following Guide sections for delivery instructions by form:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery instructions of forms for Authorized Employees on the Seller COI
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery instruction for Form 483 Section 2201.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery instructions for Form 1132 (For Servicers) Section 2405.1(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery instructions for Form 1132 (For Sellers) Section 6303.2(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery instructions for Form 987E Section 6305.3(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery instructions for Form 1132A Section 8303.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Loan Selling Advisor access and financial reimbursement requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A new Seller/Servicer will not be given access to Loan Selling Advisor until Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: has accepted the Seller/Servicer’s COI and all other required forms and documents. A
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer will not receive expense reimbursements and incentive payments via ACH credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: entries into their commercial accounts until Freddie Mac has accepted the Servicer’s COI
elements.label: text

elements.text: and all other required forms and documents, including Form 1132.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) COI renewal requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Seller/Servicer must renew and update its COI every two years and its Resolution
elements.label: text

elements.text: every four years and determine if such renewal requires new or modified Wire Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instructions. Freddie Mac will notify the Seller/Servicer of the renewal approximately
elements.label: text

elements.text: 120 days before the renewal Due Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Seller/Servicer remains responsible for timely renewal even if Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not provide notification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii) COI non-compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Seller/Servicer fails to deliver an acceptable COI by the due date specified by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, Freddie Mac has the right, in its sole discretion, to do the following until a renewal
elements.label: text

elements.text: COI has been accepted by Freddie Mac and any other Freddie Mac requirements have
elements.label: text

elements.text: been met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disable the Seller/Servicer’s access to Loan Selling Advisor and/or cancel or
elements.label: text

elements.text: postpone any pending Settlement Date or Funding Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a Servicer, cancel or postpone receipt of expense reimbursements and incentive
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments via ACH credit entries
elements.label: text

elements.text: (viii) Indemnity obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer shall indemnify and hold Freddie Mac, its directors, officers,
elements.label: text

elements.text: employees, successors, assigns and fiscal and transfer agents harmless from and against
elements.label: text

elements.text: the consequences of any such cancelation or postponement by Freddie Mac, as set forth
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 6301.4(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Related Third Parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Documentation requirements for Related Third Parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Seller/Servicer shall require each of its Related Third Parties that are to be paid by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac to sign and complete Form 99V, Certificate of Incumbency for Vendors,
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to submitting any requests for expense reimbursements or other payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before a Related Third Party may begin receiving expense reimbursements and incentive
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments, the Related Third Party must have:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completed Form 99V
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Met the process requirements herein
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Received Freddie Mac acceptance of the Related Third Party’s COI, other required
elements.label: text

elements.text: forms and documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Authorization for payment processing
elements.label: text

elements.text: To receive expense reimbursement and incentive payments, Related Third Parties must
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorize Freddie Mac to make such payments and receive debits via Wire Instructions or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ACH into their commercial checking accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Vendors must complete Form 98, Authorization for the Automatic Transfer of Funds for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Vendors, and submit the form to Freddie Mac as an Electronic Record using a PDF (or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other Electronic Record format commonly used in the mortgage industry) that has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed and contains the copy or representation of the pen and ink signature of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorized Employee (such copy or representation of the Authorized Employee’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: signature shall be such Authorized Employee’s adopted Electronic Signature) attached to
elements.label: text

elements.text: an e-mail and delivered to Freddie Mac at
elements.label: text

elements.text: Counterparty_Authorization@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2201.2: Establishing and maintaining wire transfer
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: instructions for cash proceeds (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintaining wire transfer instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prerequisites for wire transfers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submissions of Form 483, Wire Transfer Authorization, for wire transfer instructions and
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Legal indemnification obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic submission of Form 483
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Participation in electronic transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Maintaining wire transfer instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: In order to make timely and accurate wire transfers of cash proceeds for the purchase of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, Freddie Mac maintains a centralized database containing individual wire
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions as provided by the Seller on Form 483.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This file is maintained and controlled in the Freddie Mac office in McLean, Virginia.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Prerequisites for wire transfers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not wire transfer any cash proceeds for the purchase of Mortgages unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: and until the Seller has submitted a valid Certificate of Incumbency (COI) using one of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following forms:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 988SF, Certificate of Incumbency: for Banks, Savings Banks, Savings and Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Associations, Credit Unions, Corporations or Limited Liability Companies, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 989SF, Certificate of Incumbency for Limited Partnerships, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: The COI must list “Authorized Employees” who are authorized and empowered to provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac with instructions (or modified instructions) to transfer funds (cash or securities)
elements.label: text

elements.text: by wire transfer on the Seller’s behalf in connection with the sale of Mortgages to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac. See Section 2201.1 for additional information regarding COI requirements.

elements.text: Chapter 2202: Document Custodian and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Document Custody
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2202.1: Document Custodian eligibility and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: requirements (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document Custodian engagement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document Custodian eligibility and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Document Custodian engagement
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Seller/Servicer number, the Seller/Servicer must contract with at least one
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian, which may be a Designated Custodian or another approved Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian chosen by the Seller/Servicer, on the Tri-Party Agreement, in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Sections 2202.1(b) and 2202.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Tri-Party Agreement limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers may enter into Tri-Party Agreements with up to 10 Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodians per Seller/Servicer number. Compensation for each Document Custodian’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: services is the sole responsibility of the Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) eMortgage requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For requirements related to eMortgages, see Chapter 1402.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Document Custodian eligibility and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must choose a Document Custodian that will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Enter into a Tri-Party Agreement with the Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 2202.2)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Meet and maintain all applicable eligibility requirements outlined in this section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Comply with:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ All Guide requirements pertaining to Notes and assignments held for Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, including, but not limited to, Sections 4101.8 and 7101.4(c) and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Notes, assignments and related documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the requirements are amended in the Guide, then the Tri-Party Agreement will
elements.label: text

elements.text: be deemed amended as necessary to conform with such amended requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ All terms of the Tri-Party Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any other requirements that Freddie Mac may specify to ensure the safety and
elements.label: text

elements.text: security or enforceability of the Notes and assignments held by the Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Such standards, including custodial performance, and such fiduciary
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibilities as may be prescribed by Freddie Mac, in its discretion, from time
elements.label: text

elements.text: to time
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the Document Custodian must notify Freddie Mac and the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: if, at any time, it fails to meet any applicable eligibility requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must have its own oversight process to monitor its Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian(s) to ensure they remain in good standing and meet Freddie Mac eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements. If at any time, the Document Custodian fails to meet any applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility requirement, the Seller/Servicer must notify Freddie Mac within one Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Day at Institutional_Eligibility@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac is not approving new Document Custodians until further notice. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility requirements stated in this chapter and elsewhere in the Guide are requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: for continuing eligibility as a Freddie Mac Document Custodian. This does not limit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: ability of Freddie Mac-approved Document Custodians to obtain Freddie Mac approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: to provide eNote custody. (See Section 1402.4 regarding eNote custodian requirements.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Basic eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: An eligible Document Custodian must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Be one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A financial institution that is supervised and regulated by the FDIC, Board of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Governors of the Federal Reserve System or the Office of the Comptroller of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Currency,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A subsidiary of a supervised and regulated financial institution listed above,
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorized to perform trust services under applicable law, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Federal Home Loan Bank chartered pursuant to the Federal Home Loan Bank
elements.label: text

elements.text: Act
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Not be in receivership, conservatorship or liquidation and may not be a subsidiary of
elements.label: text

elements.text: a federally regulated parent that is in receivership, conservatorship or liquidation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Maintain financial suitability by either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintaining an acceptable risk threshold based on the requirements set forth in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 2202.1(b)(iii) or (iv), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Having an Acceptable Net Worth of at least $500,000,000 if it is one of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ An entity applying to be a Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Document Custodian with existing custodial relationship(s) or entering into
elements.label: text

elements.text: new custodial relationships with Freddie Mac Seller/Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers and Document Custodians will be notified if a Document Custodian is
elements.label: text

elements.text: below the acceptable risk threshold and does not have an Acceptable Net Worth of at
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Be equipped with secure, fire-resistant storage facilities with adequate controls on
elements.label: text

elements.text: access to ensure the safety and security of the Notes and assignments in its custody.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The vault must provide a minimum of two hours fire protection, and the fireproof
elements.label: text

elements.text: certificate must be renewed annually; however, if a Document Custodian’s regulator
elements.label: text

elements.text: requires a higher standard of fire protection, then that higher standard shall apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Maintain the Notes and assignments in such a manner as to ensure security and
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidentiality and to prohibit unauthorized access to or use of information contained
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Notes and assignments
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Use employees who are knowledgeable in the handling of Notes and assignments and
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the functions and duties of a Document Custodian as required by Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including access to and use of Loan Selling Advisor®. (See Section 2403.2(b) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: information on obtaining Loan Selling Advisor access and user roles.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Access the electronic version of the Guide through the link on
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://guide.freddiemac.com or arrange for a current subscription to the electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide via AllRegs®
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Maintain the following insurance coverages, at a minimum:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Financial institution bond, or equivalent insurance, covering any loss resulting
elements.label: text

elements.text: from:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Employee dishonesty,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Physical damage or destruction to, or loss of, any Notes and assignments
elements.label: text

elements.text: while such documents are located on the Document Custodian’s premises, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Physical damage or destruction to, or loss of, any Notes and assignments
elements.label: text

elements.text: while such documents are in transit between the Document Custodian’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: premises and any destination, regardless of the means by which they are
elements.label: text

elements.text: transported, if the Document Custodian has contractually agreed with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer to assume liability for Notes and assignments while in transit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 2202.3(b)(iii) for Seller/Servicer insurance requirements if
elements.label: text

elements.text: the parties have not so agreed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Errors and omissions insurance covering claims resulting from the Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian’s breach of duty, neglect, errors or omissions, misstatements,
elements.label: text

elements.text: misleading statements or other wrongful acts committed in the conduct of its
elements.label: text

elements.text: services.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the purpose of these insurance coverages, the Notes are to be defined as
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Negotiable Instruments” per Section 3-104 of the Uniform Commercial Code.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s insurance requirements as stated above do not diminish, restrict or
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise limit the Document Custodian’s responsibilities and obligations as stated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: any Tri-Party Agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The required insurance coverages must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be underwritten by an insurer that has an A− (A minus) or better rating according
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the A.M. Best Company,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be maintained in amounts that are deemed adequate for the number of Notes held
elements.label: text

elements.text: in custody and appropriate based on prudent business practice, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each have a deductible amount of no more than the greater of 5% of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian’s generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) net
elements.label: text

elements.text: worth or $100,000 but in no case be more than $10,000,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Document Custodian may be covered under its parent’s insurance program rather
elements.label: text

elements.text: than maintaining its own insurance only if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The acceptable deductible amount for each insurance coverage does not exceed
elements.label: text

elements.text: the greater of 5% of the parent’s GAAP net worth or $100,000 but in no case be
elements.label: text

elements.text: more than $10,000,000,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Document Custodian is a named insured, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The parent’s insurance policy(ies) meets the insurance requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodians as stated in this section
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event of cancelation or non-renewal of any of the required insurance coverages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Document Custodian or its insurer, insurance broker or agent must provide the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac’s Counterparty Credit Risk Management Department
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Directory 1) 30 days advance written notice thereof.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. Have and maintain a document tracking and reporting system that, at minimum:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provides, in an electronic format acceptable to Freddie Mac, an accounting of all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notes held for Freddie Mac identified by Freddie Mac loan number and the six￾digit Seller/Servicer number,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monitors the receipt of Notes and assignments, including related or trailing
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation (e.g., modifying instruments or powers of attorney),
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monitors the release of Notes and assignments,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tracks the physical location of Notes and related documents,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cross-references the Freddie Mac loan number for each Mortgage with the loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: number assigned by the Seller/Servicer, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accurately accounts for documents transferred or released
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Document Custodian must provide screen prints of its document tracking system
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac with its request for initial approval annually as part of its eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: certification and upon Freddie Mac’s request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10. Have and maintain a disaster recovery plan that documents, at a minimum:
elements.label: text
