elements.text: ■ The process by which the physical recovery/restoration of documents will occur,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The recovery of tracking system data, including any electronically maintained
elements.label: text

elements.text: information,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The relocation/restoration of the facilities to ensure continuing ability to perform
elements.label: text

elements.text: required custodial functions,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provisions for the testing and maintenance of the plan, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A provision to notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 9) of a disaster within 24 hours
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the disaster according to the requirement in Section 8107.2(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 11. Have and implement written procedures that ensure compliance with Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements and prudent practices in performing the duties of a Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: with respect to the Notes and associated documents, including, at minimum:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Certification and maintenance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Release and transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Access
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tracking and reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Eligibility requirements for the Seller/Servicer acting as its own Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subject to Freddie Mac’s approval and in Freddie Mac’s sole discretion, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer may act as its own Document Custodian if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All requirements in Sections 2202.1(b)(i) and (ii) are satisfied, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Notes and assignments for Mortgages serviced for Freddie Mac in its custody are
elements.label: text

elements.text: entrusted to a department that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Is established and operated under trust powers granted by the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: primary regulator
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Has custodial officers who are duly authorized to act on behalf of the Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian in its trust capacity and empowered to enter into a Tri-Party Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Is subject to periodic review, examination and inspection by the regulator granting
elements.label: text

elements.text: trust powers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ It has a financial rating of 130 or better from IDC Financial Publishing, Inc. (IDC)
elements.label: text

elements.text: or a C rating or better from Kroll Bond Rating Agency, Inc. (KBRA). Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian may satisfy one rating provided the other rating is not lower than 75
elements.label: text

elements.text: from IDC or a C from KBRA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Document Custodian is not a rated institution, then Document Custodian’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: parent or subsidiary must meet these standards.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the Seller/Servicer is required to have a financial rating that meets
elements.label: text

elements.text: or exceeds a long-term rating of BBB from Standard & Poor’s or a long-term
elements.label: text

elements.text: rating of Baa2 from Moody’s. If the Seller/Servicer is not a rated institution, then
elements.label: text

elements.text: the nearest parent must meet these requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Is independently and separately managed from any functional area that performs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage origination, selling or Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Maintains separate records, files and operations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Uses personnel not engaged in the functions of Mortgage origination, selling or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing to perform the custodial function
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Eligibility requirements for a third-party Document Custodian that is not an
elements.label: text

elements.text: affiliate of the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: An institution that is not an affiliate of the Seller/Servicer may act as a Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ It meets all requirements in Sections 2202.1(b)(i) and (ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ It has a financial rating of 125 or better from IDC or a C rating or better from KBRA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian may satisfy one rating provided the other rating is not lower
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 75 from IDC or a C from KBRA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Document Custodian is not a rated institution, then Document Custodian’s parent
elements.label: text

elements.text: or subsidiary must meet these standards.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Within the institution, the document custodial function:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Is independently and separately managed from any functional area that performs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage origination, selling or Servicing,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Maintains separate records, files and operations, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Is performed by personnel not engaged in the functions of Mortgage origination,
elements.label: text

elements.text: selling or Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Eligibility requirements for a third-party Document Custodian that is an affiliate of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subject to Freddie Mac’s approval and in Freddie Mac’s sole discretion, a third-party that
elements.label: text

elements.text: is an affiliate of the Seller/Servicer may act as a Document Custodian if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ It meets all the requirements in Sections 2202.1(b)(i) and (ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ It is independently and separately managed from the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The third-party Document Custodian may occupy the same premises as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer, as long as the Seller/Servicer is not involved in the management or
elements.label: text

elements.text: operations of the third-party custodian.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ It has a financial rating of 130 or better from IDC or a C rating or better from KBRA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian may satisfy one rating provided the other rating is not lower
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 75 from IDC or a C from KBRA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Document Custodian is not a rated institution, then Document Custodian’s parent
elements.label: text

elements.text: or subsidiary must meet these standards.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the Seller/Servicer is required to have a financial rating that meets or
elements.label: text

elements.text: exceeds a long-term rating of BBB from Standard & Poor’s or a long-term rating of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Baa2 from Moody’s. If the Seller/Servicer is not a rated institution, then the nearest
elements.label: text

elements.text: parent must meet these requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Within the institution, the document custodial function:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Is independently and separately managed from any functional area that performs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage origination, selling or Servicing,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Maintains separate records, files and operations, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Is performed by personnel not engaged in the functions of Mortgage origination,
elements.label: text

elements.text: selling or Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Additional eligibility requirements for a Document Custodian that is an affiliate of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Warehouse Lender
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac recognizes that there may be instances where, for operational efficiency, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: document custodial function shares personnel with the institution’s warehouse lending
elements.label: text

elements.text: function. This sharing of personnel will be allowed only when the document custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: function has:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A separate tracking and reporting system that provides a clear distinction between
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s assets and the collateral held for the Warehouse Lender,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Separate record keeping from other functional areas, including warehouse lending,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Operating controls that provide a clear distinction between:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Activities that an employee performs for the benefit of the Warehouse Lender and
elements.label: text

elements.text: activities performed for Freddie Mac, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Management decisions that apply to collateral held as security for the warehouse
elements.label: text

elements.text: line and those that apply to Notes that are held in trust for the sole benefit of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2202.2: Contracting with a Document Custodian
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contracting with a Document Custodian other than a Designated Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contracting with a Designated Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special requirements – Mortgages sold through Cash-Released XChange®
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special requirements for eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Terminating the document custodial relationship
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Contracting with a Document Custodian other than a Designated Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Submission of Form 1035, Document Custodial Agreement: Single-Family
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before delivering any Notes and assignments to a Document Custodian other than a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Designated Custodian, a paper version of Form 1035 executed by the Seller/Servicer and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Document Custodian must be delivered to and approved by Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 1).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1035 is the Tri-Party Agreement among Freddie Mac, a Document Custodian and a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer governing the verification and custody of all Notes and assignments for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages delivered for sale to or serviced for Freddie Mac by a Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Additional application requirements to become a Freddie Mac-approved Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian may be obtained from Freddie Mac (see Directory 1).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Freddie Mac approval and assignments
elements.label: text

elements.text: After reviewing the Document Custodian application and any other required
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation, if Freddie Mac determines that the applicant meets the eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements, Freddie Mac will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assign a Document Custodian number to the Document Custodian,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assign a Tri-Party Agreement number to identify that relationship,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Execute Form 1035,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide the Seller/Servicer and the Document Custodian each with a copy of the fully
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed Form 1035,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Inform the respective parties of the Document Custodian number and the Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial agreement number, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain the original fully executed Form 1035
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Document Custodian will be assigned only one Document Custodian number for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer relationships approved at that location. It must, however, complete a
elements.label: text

elements.text: separate application, including a Form 1035 executed by the Seller/Servicer and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian, for each Seller/Servicer that wishes to use its custodial services. A
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian with an assigned Document Custodian number must insert the
elements.label: text

elements.text: number in the appropriate space on Form 1035.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Delivery restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers may not make a delivery pursuant to Section 6301.4(a) until Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: has:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Executed the Form 1035, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provided written confirmation of the approval and its effective date
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Audit and compliance checks
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Document Custodians other than the Designated Custodians, Freddie Mac relies on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the representations and warranties of the Seller/Servicer in Form 1035 that the Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian satisfies the eligibility requirements of Section 2202.1(b). Freddie Mac and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: its auditors:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ May perform, with or without prior notice, on-site audits of all records and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents held by the Document Custodian that relate to the Notes and assignments
elements.label: text

elements.text: held for Freddie Mac, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reserve the right to perform on-site audits at any time to evaluate the Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian’s compliance with Freddie Mac requirements and to assess its internal
elements.label: text

elements.text: controls
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s execution of Form 1035 will not be deemed to be an approval of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian or a waiver of any rights to enforce the eligibility conditions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Contracting with a Designated Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Establishing custodial relationship with Designated Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may use a Freddie Mac Designated Custodian to hold the Notes for Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: they service for Freddie Mac, except as set forth in Sections 2202.2(c) and (d).
elements.label: text

elements.text: To establish a custodial relationship with a Designated Custodian, the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must complete:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ A Tri-Party Agreement on Form 1035DC, Designated Custodial Agreement: Single￾Family Mortgages, for BNYM or Form 1035CS, Designated Custodial Agreement:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Single-Family Mortgages, for Computershare Trust Company, N.A.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (“Computershare”), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ All other forms required by, and available from, the Designated Custodian (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 4)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note that Forms 1035DC and 1035CS differ from Form 1035.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Freddie Mac assignments and notifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac has assigned each Designated Custodian a Document Custodian number to
elements.label: text

elements.text: insert on the application package and in the Tri-Party Agreement. Freddie Mac will
elements.label: text

elements.text: review each application and, if approved, will notify the Seller/Servicer of the effective
elements.label: text

elements.text: date of the related Tri-Party Agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Questions about the Designated Custodians and Form 1035DC or Form 1035CS should
elements.label: text

elements.text: be directed to Freddie Mac (see Directory 1).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Questions about BNYMs Designated Custodian Registration Forms or Computershare’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: customer forms should be directed to the appropriate entity (see Directory 4).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Special requirements – Mortgages sold through Cash-Released XChange
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Mortgages other than eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages other than eMortgages, Sellers selling Mortgages to Freddie Mac through
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash-Released XChange, and Servicers participating in that process, must use a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian set forth in Exhibit 43, Cash-Released XChange® Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodians.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Document Custodian used by the Seller/Servicer is a Designated Custodian, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 2202.2(b) apply; in all other cases, the Seller/Servicer should
elements.label: text

elements.text: refer to the requirements in Section 2202.2(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For deliveries and Transfers of Servicing of eMortgages sold through Cash-Released
elements.label: text

elements.text: XChange, Seller/Servicers must use Freddie Mac as the eNote Custodian (see Directory
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4 and Chapter 1402).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Special requirements for eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers selling or Servicing eMortgages must comply with the document custody
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Chapter 1402 and the Seller’s other Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Terminating the document custodial relationship
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any party may terminate a Tri-Party Agreement as set forth in Section 2202.4. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must transfer all Notes and assignments for Mortgages held pursuant to the agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: being terminated to a Transferee Document Custodian as described in Sections 2202.4
elements.label: text

elements.text: and 7101.4(c), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ May not refuse, or fail to fulfill its custodial responsibilities or obligations under the Tri￾Party Agreement being terminated until all Notes and assignments have been received by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Transferee Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2202.3: Document Custodian duties and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: responsibilities (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document Custodian duties to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller/Servicer and the Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Document Custodian duties to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian covenants to Freddie Mac that it will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Maintain physical custody of all Mortgage documents delivered including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Original paper Notes,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Addendums, riders, assignments,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Powers of attorney and modification agreements, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Cooperative Share Loans, the documents listed in Section 6304.2(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Such documents include any copies provided to the Document Custodian in trust for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: benefit of Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Original executed paper document (other than a Note) – electronically recorded:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Seller/Servicer must deliver the original executed paper document and a
elements.label: text

elements.text: paper copy of such electronically recorded document (showing its recordation
elements.label: text

elements.text: information), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Document Custodian will maintain such original executed paper document
elements.label: text

elements.text: and such paper copy of the electronically recorded original paper document
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronically created document (other than a Note) – electronically recorded:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Seller/Servicer must deliver a paper copy of such electronically recorded
elements.label: text

elements.text: document (containing its recordation information), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Document Custodian will maintain such paper copy of the electronically
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorded Electronic (as defined in Section 1401.1(b)) document
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronically created document (other than a Note) – not required by State law to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorded:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Seller/Servicer must deliver a paper copy of the unrecorded electronically
elements.label: text

elements.text: created document, certified by the Seller/Servicer as a true and correct copy, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Document Custodian will maintain such certified paper copy of the document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Sections 1401.3(d) and 1401.3(e) for Seller/Servicer delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: pertaining to Electronic recording of closing and post-closing documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Make available for review by Freddie Mac or its designee, at any time during normal
elements.label: text

elements.text: business hours, with or without prior notice:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notes and assignments and the facilities in which they are stored,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintenance and release procedures,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Control and tracking systems, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other evidence of compliance with eligibility requirements as requested
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Make the custodial staff available for interview by Freddie Mac or its designee at any
elements.label: text

elements.text: time during normal business hours, with or without prior notice, for an assessment of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: staff’s familiarity with and adherence to Freddie Mac’s custodial requirements and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian’s internal controls
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Indemnify Freddie Mac for such losses as may occur as a result of any negligence by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian in the performance of its duties under the Guide and the Tri-Party
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement pertaining to Freddie Mac Notes and assignments in the custody of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Maintain physical custody of the Note and assignments, in trust, for the benefit of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer in circumstances when the Document Custodian receives a completed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: signed Form 1036, Request for Possession or Control of Documents, from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer requesting constructive possession
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Provide, in an electronic format acceptable to Freddie Mac, an accounting of all Notes
elements.label: text

elements.text: and assignments, as described in Section 2202.1(b)(ii)(9), that the Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: has in its physical custody:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) In trust for the benefit of Freddie Mac, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) In trust for the benefit of the Seller/Servicer when the Seller/Servicer has constructive
elements.label: text

elements.text: possession of the Notes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Consent and agree to conduct Electronic Transactions, as defined in Chapter 1401, with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac in connection with its functions, duties and
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations under this section and Form 1035, Document Custodial Agreement: Single￾Family Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Adopt, as its signature and its Electronic Signature (as defined in Section 1401.1(b)), its
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Document Custodian number and comply with the applicable requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Chapter 1401 as if each reference to the word “Seller/Servicer” were a reference to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: words “Document Custodian” and/or “Designated Custodian,” as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may, at any time, and in its sole discretion, require a Document Custodian to
elements.label: text

elements.text: segregate the Notes and assignments that it maintains, in trust, for the benefit of Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: from those it maintains for other investors.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Document Custodian may not enter into any understanding, agreement or relationship
elements.label: text

elements.text: with any party by which any such party would obtain, retain or claim any interest (including
elements.label: text

elements.text: an ownership or security interest) in the Notes and any assignments or the underlying
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, unless otherwise specifically and expressly approved by Freddie Mac in writing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Document Custodian’s facilities are affected by a disaster, the Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: must promptly notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 9), but such notification shall occur no
elements.label: text

elements.text: later than 24 hours after the discovery of such disaster, time being of the essence.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Seller/Servicer and the Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Responsibility for documents and Document Custodian compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer agrees to indemnify Freddie Mac and hold Freddie Mac harmless for
elements.label: text

elements.text: any loss, damage or expense (including court costs and reasonable attorney fees) that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may incur as a result of the Seller/Servicer’s Document Custodian holding
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notes and any other documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is responsible at all times for the original Note. If a Note is lost or destroyed,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must follow the procedures set forth in Section 8107.1(d).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: For all Document Custodians, except with respect to the Designated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodians, the Seller/Servicer is responsible for verifying that its Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: complies with all applicable Freddie Mac requirements regarding Note custody.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Freddie Mac’s Document Custody Procedures Handbook is located and available to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers and Document Custodians on the internet at AllRegs.com, or at
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/working-with-us/selling-delivery/delivery-options￾pricing/document-custody. Seller/Servicers and Document Custodians will find this
elements.label: text

elements.text: handbook to be a useful resource in fulfilling these requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Monitoring the eligibility status of the Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer is responsible for monitoring its Document Custodian for compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Freddie Mac’s Document Custodian eligibility requirements and must ensure that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Document Custodian complies with all eligibility requirements at all times, with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: exception of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Designated Custodians, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Certification and verification services performed outside of the United States by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodians
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will monitor the Designated Custodians and any activities of Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodians that Freddie Mac has contractually authorized to be conducted overseas.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, at any time, the Document Custodian (including a Designated Custodian) fails to
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with any eligibility requirement or violates a provision of the Guide or Tri-Party
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement, the Seller/Servicer must contact Freddie Mac (see Directory 1) in writing
elements.label: text

elements.text: within one day of the Seller/Servicer learning of the noncompliance. Freddie Mac, in its
elements.label: text

elements.text: discretion, may allow the Seller/Servicer a period of time to work with its Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian to ensure that the Document Custodian takes all necessary steps to meet the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to immediately terminate a Tri-Party Agreement. Further,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may direct the Seller/Servicer to transfer the Notes to a Designated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian or a new Document Custodian pursuant to Sections 2202.1 through 2202.2 and
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer all Notes and assignments for Mortgages serviced for Freddie Mac from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: previous Document Custodian to the new Document Custodian, pursuant to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Transit insurance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless the Servicer has agreed with the Document Custodian in writing for the Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian to assume liability for Notes and assignments while in transit, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must obtain insurance covering physical damage or destruction to, or loss of, any Notes
elements.label: text

elements.text: and assignments while such documents are in transit between the Document Custodian’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: vault and anywhere, regardless of the means by which they are transported.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the purpose of this insurance, Mortgage Notes are to be referred to as “Negotiable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instruments” as that term is defined in Section 3-104 of the model Uniform Commercial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Code (UCC), promulgated by the National Conference of Commissioners on Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: State Laws and enacted in the applicable State.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Minimum insurance coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: At a minimum, the required insurance coverage must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be underwritten by an insurer that has an A− (A minus) or better rating according
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the A.M. Best Company,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be maintained in amounts that are deemed adequate for the number of Notes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignments held in custody and that are deemed appropriate based on prudent
elements.label: text

elements.text: business practice,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each have a deductible amount no more than the greater of 5% of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) net worth or
elements.label: text

elements.text: $100,000, but in no case greater than $10,000,000, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not exclude from coverage Negotiable Instruments that are, under Article 3 of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: UCC, bearer paper (e.g., Notes with blank endorsements) with an endorsement
elements.label: text

elements.text: that does not identify a payee
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Coverage under parent’s insurance program
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer is covered under its parent’s insurance program rather than by its own
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The acceptable deductible amount for each insurance coverage may be no more
elements.label: text

elements.text: than the greater of 5% of the parent’s GAAP net worth or $100,000, but in no
elements.label: text

elements.text: case greater than $10,000,000,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must be a named insured, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The parent’s insurance policy(ies) must meet requirements as stated in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event of cancelation or non-renewal of any of the required insurance coverages, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer or the Servicer’s insurer, insurance broker or agent must provide Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Directory 1) a minimum of 30 days advance written notice thereof.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s insurance requirements as stated in this section do not diminish, restrict or
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise limit the Servicer’s responsibilities and obligations as stated in the Tri-Party
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement or otherwise in the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2202.4: Transfer of custody and terminating Tri-Party
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Agreements (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfers and terminations initiated by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfers and terminations initiated by Seller/Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Terminations initiated by Document Custodians
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Transfers and terminations initiated by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Termination without cause
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon 30 days written notice to the Seller/Servicer and the Document Custodian and at its
elements.label: text

elements.text: sole discretion, Freddie Mac may terminate a Tri-Party Agreement without cause and
elements.label: text

elements.text: require the transfer of all Notes and assignments held by the Document Custodian to
elements.label: text

elements.text: another Document Custodian or to a Designated Custodian (see Directory 4).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Immediate termination conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may immediately terminate a Tri-Party Agreement upon written notice to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer and the Document Custodian and require the transfer of all Notes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignments to another Document Custodian or as directed by Freddie Mac upon the
elements.label: text

elements.text: occurrence of any of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disqualification or suspension of the Seller/Servicer pursuant to Section 2301.1(b),
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Failure of the Document Custodian to meet Freddie Mac’s eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Section 2202.1(b)),
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s determination that the Document Custodian’s performance has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: unsatisfactory, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other circumstance with respect to the Document Custodian, the Notes or
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignments that might adversely affect the Notes or assignments or Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: interests
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Transfers and terminations initiated by Seller/Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: To change Document Custodians for a group of Mortgages or to terminate a Tri-Party
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement, the Seller/Servicer must ensure the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ It has a new Tri-Party Agreement in place with another Document Custodian (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 2202.1(b) and 2202.2), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Document Custodian transfers custody of all Notes and assignments held pursuant
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Tri-Party Agreement being terminated to the Transferee Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: within 30 days after Freddie Mac’s written approval of the transfer of custody or by such
elements.label: text

elements.text: other date requested by the Seller/Servicer and approved by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Freddie Mac may require the Notes and assignments to be transferred immediately.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Terminations initiated by Document Custodians
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Document Custodian may terminate a Tri-Party Agreement by following these required
elements.label: text

elements.text: steps:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide 90 days prior written notice to the Seller/Servicer and to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 1), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Document Custodian must transfer custody of all Notes and assignments held
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to the Tri-Party Agreement being terminated to a new Document Custodian no
elements.label: text

elements.text: later than 30 days after Freddie Mac’s written approval of transfer of custody or by such
elements.label: text

elements.text: other date approved by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Freddie Mac may require the Notes and assignments to be transferred immediately.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Additional responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Fulfillment of obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Document Custodian may not refuse or fail to fulfill its custodial obligations under a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tri-Party Agreement or for any Mortgage in a transfer of custody until the Notes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignments have been transferred to the Transferee Document Custodian.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Certification of transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Transferee Document Custodian must deliver Form 1034T, Document Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certification Schedule for Transfer of Custody and Subsequent Transfers of Servicing,
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed by the Seller/Servicer and the Transferee Document Custodian to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Directory 9) no later than 180 days after the termination date of the Tri-Party
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement or approved Date of Transfer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: By submitting Form 1034T to Freddie Mac, the Seller/Servicer and the Transferee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian represent and warrant to Freddie Mac that the information in Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1034T is correct and complete and that each of them shall be bound by the certifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: contained in Form 1034T.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 2203: MERS® Membership
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 2203.1: MERS® membership (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ MERS® membership
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Termination of MERS membership
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) MERS membership
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must comply with the requirements of the MERS Governing Documents if
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer is a MERS Member and sells to and/or services on behalf of Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as applicable, Mortgages registered on the MERS System.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If any requirements of the MERS Governing Documents conflict with the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Guide, the Seller/Servicer must comply with the requirements of the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Termination of MERS membership
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Seller/Servicer’s membership in MERS is terminated for any reason, the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must promptly notify Freddie Mac by e-mailing MERS@FreddieMac.com with the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: line “MERS Membership Termination.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Mortgage registered on the MERS System that will be sold to or is being serviced
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Freddie Mac, the Seller/Servicer must, upon such termination and as applicable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If retaining Servicing:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Prepare an assignment of the Mortgage from MERS to itself,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Have the assignment executed, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Where required by law, record the executed assignment in the applicable public land
elements.label: text

elements.text: records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If transferring Servicing to a Transferee Servicer, follow the Concurrent Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing obligations under Section 6301.2(d)(iv). The requirements will depend on
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the Transferee Servicer is a MERS Member.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 2301: Disqualification or Suspension of
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: a Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2301.1: Disqualification or suspension of Seller’s
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: eligibility to sell (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disqualification or suspension of Seller’s eligibility to sell
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disqualification or suspension of the Seller or the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notice of intent to disqualify or suspend the Seller or the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Disqualification or suspension of Seller’s eligibility to sell
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, in its discretion, may disqualify or suspend a Seller from eligibility to sell
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages to Freddie Mac. The Seller will be ineligible to obtain new purchase commitments
elements.label: text

elements.text: during a period of disqualification or suspension.
elements.label: text

elements.text: During the period of Seller disqualification or suspension, Freddie Mac may, in its discretion,
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine whether outstanding commitments held by the Seller must be honored or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: outstanding balance of the commitment fulfilled by using the pairoff procedure (see Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will determine the length of any suspension period and may prescribe the terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: and conditions for reinstatement. The notice of disqualification or suspension and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: opportunity of a Seller to respond to and appeal the action are governed by the procedures in
elements.label: text

elements.text: this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For information regarding outstanding purchase commitments when Freddie Mac terminates
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights with or without cause, see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 3602.2(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Disqualification or suspension of the Seller or the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Disqualification or suspension with cause
elements.label: text

elements.text: Without limiting Freddie Mac’s right to take whatever other action it deems appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: to protect its interests and enforce its rights (including disqualification or suspension for
elements.label: text

elements.text: reasons not listed below), Freddie Mac may disqualify or suspend a Seller or a Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: for any of the following reasons:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Impending or actual insolvency of the Seller/Servicer, its parent, an affiliate or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsidiary
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Filing of a voluntary petition by the Seller/Servicer, its parent, or an affiliate or
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsidiary under federal bankruptcy or State insolvency laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Filing of an answer by the Seller/Servicer, its parent, an affiliate or a subsidiary in an
elements.label: text

elements.text: involuntary proceeding admitting insolvency or inability to pay debts
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Adjudication of the Seller/Servicer, its parent, an affiliate or a subsidiary as bankrupt
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Appointment of a trustee or receiver for the Seller/Servicer or its property or for its
elements.label: text

elements.text: parent, an affiliate or a subsidiary or for the property of such entities
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Execution by the Seller/Servicer, its parent, an affiliate or a subsidiary of an
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignment for the benefit of creditors
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Failure of the Seller/Servicer, its parent, or affiliate or a subsidiary to obtain a
elements.label: text

elements.text: vacation or stay of involuntary proceedings brought for its reorganization, dissolution
elements.label: text

elements.text: or liquidation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Failure of the Seller/Servicer to maintain qualified loan origination or Servicing staff
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or adequate facilities to assure (i) the investment quality of the Mortgages sold to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, or (ii) the adequacy of the Servicing of Mortgages purchased by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. Any weakness or notable change in the financial or organizational status or
elements.label: text

elements.text: management of the Seller/Servicer, its parent, an affiliate or a subsidiary, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: any adverse change in profitability or liquidity, that, in the opinion of Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: could adversely affect Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10. Failure of the Seller/Servicer to meet any requirement as may be prescribed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac for eligibility as a Seller or a Servicer, including, but not limited to, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s failure to maintain a minimum Tangible Net Worth and/or failure to
elements.label: text

elements.text: meet any other financial requirements related to the Seller/Servicer’s eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: required by Freddie Mac (see Section 2101.2)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 11. Placement of the Seller/Servicer, its parent, an affiliate or a subsidiary on probation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: restriction of its activities or the activities of its parent, an affiliate or a subsidiary of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer, in any manner by a federal or State government agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: 12. Any judgment, order, finding or regulatory action to which the Seller/Servicer (or its
elements.label: text

elements.text: management), its parent, an affiliate or a subsidiary is subject that would adversely
elements.label: text

elements.text: affect the Seller/Servicer’s ability to comply with the terms and conditions of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: 13. Freddie Mac’s determination that the Seller/Servicer’s warranty obligations are
elements.label: text

elements.text: disproportionate to its capital and/or assets
elements.label: text

elements.text: 14. Failure of the Seller to deliver any documents under a Freddie Mac mandatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivery purchase program
elements.label: text

elements.text: 15. Failure of the Seller/Servicer to observe or comply with any term or provision of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: 16. Misstatement, misrepresentation or omission of any material fact on any application,
elements.label: text

elements.text: certification or other document submitted, or in any oral representation made, by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: 17. Assignment or attempt to assign its interests, rights or obligations under the Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents by the Seller/Servicer without Freddie Mac’s written consent
elements.label: text

elements.text: 18. Failure or inability to account properly for the disposition of all moneys required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be safeguarded in Custodial Accounts or buydown accounts by the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 19. Default or failure of the Seller/Servicer to perform under any contract with Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, including, but not limited to, contracts not directly related to the sale or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing of Mortgages and contracts relating to Mortgages purchased or serviced
elements.label: text

elements.text: other than pursuant to this Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: 20. Having a 30-, 60- or 90+-day Delinquency rate or an REO rate more than 50% higher
elements.label: text

elements.text: than the average 30-, 60- or 90+-day Delinquency rate or REO rate for any or all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages owned or guaranteed by Freddie Mac nationally or in the same
elements.label: text

elements.text: geographical area (which may include Standard Metropolitan Statistical Area, county
elements.label: text

elements.text: or State) in which the Mortgaged Premises that secure the Mortgages either sold by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller or serviced by the Servicer are located and with similar Mortgage and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower characteristics, such as origination year, loan-to-value ratio and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation type (e.g., full documentation, reduced documentation)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 21. Failure of the Seller/Servicer to submit a complete and accurate Annual Certification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report within the time frame prescribed by Section 2101.11
elements.label: text

elements.text: 22. Failure of the Seller/Servicer that is a mortgage banker to submit a complete and
elements.label: text

elements.text: accurate Form 1055, Mortgage Bankers’ Financial Reporting Form, within the time
elements.label: text

elements.text: frame prescribed by Section 2101.5
elements.label: text

elements.text: 23. Failure of the Seller/Servicer to comply with the provisions of Section 3101.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding the Freddie Mac Exclusionary List
elements.label: text

elements.text: 24. Placement on the Exclusionary List of the Seller/Servicer, its parent, an affiliate or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsidiary
elements.label: text

elements.text: 25. Failure of the Seller/Servicer to submit a complete and accurate Change and Activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report within the time frame required in Section 2101.12 or 2101.13
elements.label: text

elements.text: 26. Freddie Mac’s determination that the Servicer’s overall performance is unacceptable
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to Section 3501.2. Freddie Mac considers the Servicer Success Scorecard
elements.label: text

elements.text: results (see Section 3501.2), together with other factors (including, but not limited to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer Success File Reviews, trends in performance, adequacy of staffing, audit
elements.label: text

elements.text: results and/or compliance with all requirements of the Purchase Documents) in
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluating whether the Servicer’s overall performance is unacceptable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 27. Failure of the Seller to deliver a minimum volume of Mortgages (measured by dollar
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount or number of Mortgages) within the time frame specified by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: 28. Failure of the Seller/Servicer to maintain the confidentiality of the Exclusionary List
elements.label: text

elements.text: or information contained on the Exclusionary List
elements.label: text

elements.text: 29. Failure of the Seller/Servicer to fulfill any obligation to Freddie Mac when due,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to, the failure to repurchase a Mortgage, pay fees or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: moneys and remit custodial or buydown funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: 30. Failure of the Seller/Servicer to provide audited or reviewed financial statements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: its parent, subsidiaries and affiliates upon Freddie Mac request
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Seller is also a Servicer, suspension or disqualification as a Seller is sufficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: cause for suspension or disqualification as a Servicer or termination of all or a portion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights with cause.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Servicer is also a Seller, suspension or disqualification as a Servicer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: termination of all or a portion of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rights with cause under Chapter 3603 is sufficient cause for suspension or
elements.label: text

elements.text: disqualification as a Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Whether or not Freddie Mac terminates the Servicing Contract and related Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract Rights, any of the above events is cause for revocation of the power of attorney
elements.label: text

elements.text: granted to the Servicer in accordance with Section 8101.1(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Disqualification or suspension without cause
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may disqualify or suspend a Seller or a Servicer without cause.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disqualification or suspension of a Seller or a Servicer without cause will be effective
elements.label: text

elements.text: immediately upon notice of the disqualification or suspension, unless the notice specifies
elements.label: text

elements.text: a later date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: During the period of Seller disqualification or suspension, Freddie Mac may, at its
elements.label: text

elements.text: discretion, determine whether outstanding commitments held by the Seller must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: honored or the outstanding balance of the commitment fulfilled by using the pairoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedure (see Section 6401.1).
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller will not be entitled to a termination fee or any other compensation from Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac for any reason or cause relating to any consequential, incidental or indirect damages
elements.label: text

elements.text: arising out of, or in connection with, its disqualification or suspension without cause.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding Sections 2301.1(c) through 2301.3, Freddie Mac’s decision to suspend
elements.label: text

elements.text: or disqualify a Seller without cause or to terminate Servicing without cause is conclusive.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Notice of intent to disqualify or suspend the Seller or the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not provide the Seller or the Servicer with prior written notice of an intent
elements.label: text

elements.text: to disqualify or suspend the Seller or the Servicer, unless Freddie Mac determines, in its
elements.label: text

elements.text: discretion, that the giving of prior notice will in no way adversely affect Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: interests. In such cases, 30 days’ prior written notice will be given.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certain violations of this Guide (such as the commission of fraudulent acts or the failure to
elements.label: text

elements.text: deliver under a mandatory delivery purchase program) are viewed with particular seriousness
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac. In such cases, Freddie Mac will tend to act without prior written notice to
elements.label: text

elements.text: disqualify or suspend the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If prior written notice is not provided, disqualification or suspension will become effective
elements.label: text

elements.text: upon oral notice from Freddie Mac to the Seller. Written confirmation of that oral notice will
elements.label: text

elements.text: follow.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any prior written notice or written confirmation of oral notice will contain a brief statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the basis for the disqualification or suspension and may advise the Seller or the Servicer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: its right to obtain a review of Freddie Mac’s action or proposed action if permitted in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the procedures in Section 2301.3(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before giving notice of disqualification or suspension, Freddie Mac may, in its discretion,
elements.label: text

elements.text: notify a Seller or a Servicer that it has violated, is violating or may be about to violate
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions of this Guide or other Purchase Documents and that, unless corrective action is
elements.label: text

elements.text: taken within a specified time period, disqualification or suspension may result.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may give the notification as part of an audit report or as a result of any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: review or investigation of the Seller or the Servicer by Freddie Mac. Any such notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be issued by means determined by Freddie Mac, including, but not limited to, electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: mail to the Seller’s or Servicer’s e-mail address provided to Freddie Mac by the Seller or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer in accordance with Freddie Mac’s requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2301.2: Request for voluntary termination of status as
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: a Freddie Mac Seller or Servicer (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submission and receipt of request
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Approval and conditions of termination
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer may ask for a voluntary termination of its status as a Freddie Mac-approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller and/or Servicer by submitting a request to Freddie Mac. The request may be submitted in
elements.label: text

elements.text: writing or submitted or transmitted in the form of a Record or Electronic Record, as those terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: are defined in Section 1401.1(b), by the Seller/Servicer’s authorized employee or representative.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the request is Electronic, it must be signed using the Seller/Servicer’s Electronic Signature and
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be subject to Chapter 1401.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Submission and receipt of request
elements.label: text

elements.text: The request will be deemed to have been duly given to and received by Freddie Mac on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: date such request is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Received in writing by Freddie Mac (see Directory 1) via first class mail
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Received in electronic form (e-mail) as an Electronic Record by Freddie Mac’s computer
elements.label: text

elements.text: information processing system via the internet at its internet e-mail address (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 1)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Received as a Record or Electronic Record in electronic form (facsimile) by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s electronic facsimile machine or system at Freddie Mac (see Directory 1)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Other addresses may be substituted for the above upon notice of the substitution.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer may also request the termination of its status as a Freddie Mac-approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller and/or Servicer by submitting a Change and Activity Report as described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Approval and conditions of termination
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer’s voluntary termination shall not be effective until an authorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: representative of Freddie Mac has approved the request in writing or in a Record or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Record. Freddie Mac may condition its approval of a voluntary termination on
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements such as full satisfaction of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FRE Claims,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The transfer of any portfolio, and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compensation to Freddie Mac for a negatively valued portfolio
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event of a portfolio transfer, the Servicer is responsible for all transfer-related expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: and remains responsible and liable to Freddie Mac for all representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: arising prior to the transfer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2301.3: Appeals and final decision (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligibility for appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Final decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligibility for appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: The provisions of this section do not apply to Sellers and Servicers approved by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: on or after October 3, 2012 to sell and/or service Mortgages. Those Sellers and Servicers do
elements.label: text

elements.text: not have a right to appeal Freddie Mac’s notice of determination to disqualify or suspend.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller or Servicer approved to sell and/or service Mortgages for Freddie Mac prior to
elements.label: text

elements.text: October 3, 2012 may write to Freddie Mac (see Directory 1) to appeal a determination to
elements.label: text

elements.text: disqualify or suspend with cause pursuant to Section 2301.1(b)(i).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Deadline for filling an appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appeal must be postmarked or hand delivered no later than 15 days:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ From the date the Seller or the Servicer received written notice of Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: intention to suspend or disqualify, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If prior written notice was not provided by Freddie Mac, from the date the Seller or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer received written confirmation of Freddie Mac’s oral notice to suspend or
elements.label: text

elements.text: disqualify
elements.label: text

elements.text: If an appeal is not filed within the 15-day period, the Seller or Servicer will be deemed to
elements.label: text

elements.text: have waived its right to appeal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Required documentation for appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any appeal must provide Freddie Mac with information and documentation that clearly
elements.label: text

elements.text: supports the Seller’s or Servicer’s request for reconsideration of Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: disqualification or suspension determination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Final decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Eligibility for appeal review
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s Vice President—Counterparty Credit Risk Management, or that person’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: designee, will review the appeal of the Seller/Servicer if eligible to file an appeal under
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 2301.3(a) before rendering a final decision.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Appeal final decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Vice President—Counterparty Credit Risk Management, or that person’s designee,
elements.label: text

elements.text: will then render a final decision in writing to the Seller/Servicer either affirming,
elements.label: text

elements.text: reversing or modifying Freddie Mac’s prior determination to disqualify or suspend.
elements.label: text

elements.text: its Documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer shall have controls in place to ensure that each Related Third Party

elements.text: and Authorized Users comply with the terms of this Master Systems License and any

elements.text: immediately.

elements.text: limiting the application of any other provision of this Master Systems License or a System￾Specific License, Freddie Mac assumes no responsibility for, and shall bear no liability

elements.text: (iii) Related Third Parties

elements.text: As permitted under Section 2401.1(c), the Seller/Servicer may engage Related Third

elements.text: Parties to assist it in its furtherance of the Purpose. Freddie Mac does not endorse any

elements.text: Related Third Party or sanction any engagement by the Seller/Servicer of any Related

elements.text: Third Party.

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer shall maintain a separate, independent contract with each Related Third
elements.label: text

elements.text: Party concerning the Seller/Servicer’s use of such party’s platform, product or service.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As part of such contract, the Seller/Servicer may appoint a Related Third Party to act as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s agent for purposes of gaining access to a System, including entering data
elements.label: text

elements.text: into the System, ordering and receiving consumer credit data through the System, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtaining Output, in each instance on behalf of its principal, the Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer shall be responsible for and bear all liability arising out of or related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: any act or omission of its Related Third Parties, including any act or omission that causes a
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Authorized User registration; access management and security; requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Excluded Servicing Tools

elements.text: (i) Authorized User registration
elements.label: text

elements.text: To facilitate its provision of System access to a Related Third Party for use on its behalf
elements.label: text

elements.text: as provided hereunder, the Seller/Servicer may share Authorized Users’ identification
elements.label: text

elements.text: codes (“User IDs”) and passwords, PIN or other access codes, including system-level
elements.label: text

elements.text: access codes (“Sys-to-Sys Credentials”), together with User IDs, the “Authentication
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credentials,” with such Related Third Party.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, Freddie Mac may provide or make available to the Seller/Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An electronic registration form for the provisioning of Authentication Credentials and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorized Users (“Form”) and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Access Manager, an automated access and credential management application
elements.label: text

elements.text: designed to facilitate the Seller/Servicer’s management of Authentication Credentials
elements.label: text

elements.text: and its Authorized Users, which may also include one or more Forms (collectively,
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Access Manager”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Access Manager
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer shall, and, if applicable, shall cause each Related Third Party to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: specifically identify each Authorized User for the System in Access Manager. Access
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manager may require the designation of one or more Authorized Users to perform
elements.label: text

elements.text: administrative functions for the particular System (each, an “Administrator”) such as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identifying other Authorized Users to whom Freddie Mac may grant System(s) access
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Receiving the Authentication Credentials from Freddie Mac; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adding or deleting Authorized Users and modifying or submitting new User IDs and
elements.label: text

elements.text: completing additional Forms in accordance with the Documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Security and safeguarding requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Without limiting the application of any information security or other similar requirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a Purchase Document including, without limitation, any contained in Chapter 1302,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer shall safeguard and protect all information it provides to Access
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manager, as well as all Authentication Credentials and other Confidential Means of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Access (as that term is defined in Section 1401.1(b)), and shall adopt security measures to
elements.label: text

elements.text: prevent the loss, theft, unauthorized access, disclosure, compromise or use of any of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foregoing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Such measures shall include the maintenance of up-to-date virus detection software to
elements.label: text

elements.text: protect against malware and other malicious software. As soon as practicable, and, in any
elements.label: text

elements.text: event, no later than within one Business Day of an Administrator’s name change, or an
elements.label: text

elements.text: employee’s or contractor’s termination, the Seller/Servicer shall either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Utilize Access Manager to register the Administrator name change, or remove or
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise delete such employee or contract worker as an Authorized User; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notify Freddie Mac of such name change or termination so that Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: register the name change or revoke the employee’s or contractor’s access to each
elements.label: text

elements.text: System
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Required notifications to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer shall notify Freddie Mac as soon as practicable and, in any event, no
elements.label: text

elements.text: later than within one Business Day in the event:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Of any actual or suspected loss, theft or unauthorized access, disclosure, compromise
elements.label: text

elements.text: or use of any System, Authentication Credential, or any other Confidential Means of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Access
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer has reason to believe that an Administrator’s access to a System,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including Access Manager, is no longer secure for any reason
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An unauthorized User has gained access to a System
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An Authorized User has gained access to a System that he or she is not authorized to
elements.label: text

elements.text: access or use; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An Authorized User has used a System for purposes other than the Purpose

elements.text: (v) Recertification and credential management
elements.label: text

elements.text: From time to time an Administrator may be required by Access Manager to engage in
elements.label: text

elements.text: campaigns to recertify the authorizations and roles of Authorized Users. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Administrator shall comply with all reasonable instructions, including any provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: through Access Manager, for any such campaign.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer shall reset Authentication Credentials no less than once every 90
elements.label: text

elements.text: days, except with respect to Sys-to-Sys Credentials, which shall be reset no less than once
elements.label: text

elements.text: every 365 days.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event such resets do not occur, or if Authentication Credentials have not been used
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a period of at least 90 days, or Sys-to-Sys Credentials have not been used for a period
elements.label: text

elements.text: of at least 365 days, then, in any such event, Freddie Mac may, in its sole discretion,
elements.label: text

elements.text: deactivate System access associated with any such Authentication Credential.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Security best practices and responsibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: Without limiting the application of any other provision of this Section 2401.1(f) or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Document, the Seller/Servicer agrees to implement and maintain industry best
elements.label: text

elements.text: practices with respect to the security of, and access to, each System, including the
elements.label: text

elements.text: imposition and enforcement of requirements against sharing of Authentication
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credentials between and among Users, and accepts all risks and liability resulting from
elements.label: text

elements.text: any failure to adopt and maintain such industry best practices.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii)Excluded Servicing Tools
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer shall manage each of its Authorized Users’ access to those Excluded
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Tools that Freddie Mac designates from time to time in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorized User registration, access management, and security requirements set forth in
elements.label: text

elements.text: this Section 2401.1(f).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer shall not permit a Related Third Party to access an Excluded
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Tool unless otherwise permitted by Freddie Mac in writing (e.g., under another
elements.label: text

elements.text: section of the Guide, other Purchase Document, applicable user agreement, system
elements.label: text

elements.text: license or related system documentation).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Until Freddie Mac designates an Excluded Servicing Tool as subject to this Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2401.1(f), including through the issuance of a Form identifying such Excluded Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tool, the Seller/Servicer must continue to follow the user access and security
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements applicable to such Excluded Servicing Tool.
